1_24_20: So she quickly emptied her jar into the trough, ran back to the well to draw water, and drew for all his camels.
1_24_21: Meanwhile, the man gazed at her in silence to know whether the LORD had made his journey prosperous or not.
1_24_22: When the camels had finished drinking, the man took a gold ring weighing half a shekel and two bracelets for her wrists weighing ten shekels of gold,
1_24_23: and said, 'Whose daughter are you? Please tell me, is there room in your father's house for us to lodge?'
1_24_24: She said to him, 'I am the daughter of Bethuel, Milcah's son, whom she bore to Nahor.'
1_24_25: She also said to him, 'We have plenty of straw and feed, and room to lodge.'
1_24_26: Then the man bowed down his head and worshiped the LORD.
1_24_27: And he said, 'Blessed be the LORD, God of my master Abraham, who has not forsaken His kindness and faithfulness toward my master. As for me, the LORD has led me to the house of my master's relatives.'
1_24_28: So the young woman ran and told her mother's household these things.
1_24_29: Now Rebekah had a brother whose name was Laban, and Laban ran out to the man at the spring.
1_24_30: When he saw the ring and the bracelets on his sister's wrists, and when he heard Rebekah's words, saying, 'Thus the man spoke to me,' he came to the man; and behold, he stood by the camels at the well.
1_24_31: And he said, 'Come in, O blessed of the LORD! Why do you stand outside? For I have prepared the house, and room for the camels.'
1_24_32: So the man came to the house. And the camels were unloaded, and straw and feed were given to the camels, and water was brought to wash his feet and the feet of the men who were with him.
1_24_33: Food was set before him to eat, but he said, 'I will not eat until I have told about my errand.' And Laban said, 'Please speak.'
1_24_34: So he said, 'I am Abraham's servant.
1_24_35: The LORD has blessed my master greatly, and he has become great; and He has given him flocks and herds, silver and gold, male and female servants, and camels and donkeys.
1_24_36: And Sarah, my master's wife, bore a son to my master when she was old; and to him he has given all that he has.
1_24_37: Now my master made me swear, saying, 'You shall not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, in whose land I dwell;
1_24_38: but you shall go to my father's house and to my family, and take a wife for my son.'
1_24_39: And I said to my master, 'Perhaps the woman will not follow me.'
1_24_40: But he said to me, 'The LORD, before whom I walk, will send His angel with you and prosper your way; and you shall take a wife for my son from my family and from my father's house.
1_24_41: You will be clear from this oath when you arrive among my family; for if they will not give her to you, then you will be released from my oath.'
1_24_42: 'And this day I came to the well and said, "O LORD, God of my master Abraham, if You will now prosper the way in which I go,
1_24_43: behold, I stand by the well of water; and it shall come to pass that when the virgin comes out to draw water, and I say to her, "Please give me a little water from your jar to drink,"
1_24_44: and she says to me, "Drink, and I will draw for your camels also,"—let her be the woman whom the LORD has appointed for my master's son.'
1_24_45: 'But before I had finished speaking in my heart, there was Rebekah, coming out with her jar on her shoulder; and she went down to the well and drew water. And I said to her, "Please let me drink.'"}
1_24_46: She quickly lowered her pitcher from her shoulder and said, 'Drink, and I will also give your camels a drink.' So I drank, and she watered the camels as well.
1_24_47: Then I asked her, 'Whose daughter are you?' She answered, 'The daughter of Bethuel, Nahor's son, whom Milcah bore to him.' So I put the ring on her nose and the bracelets on her wrists.
1_24_48: Then I bowed my head and worshiped the LORD, and blessed the LORD, the God of my master Abraham, who had led me by the right way to take the daughter of my master's brother for his son.
1_24_49: Now if you will show kindness and faithfulness to my master, tell me; and if not, tell me, that I may turn to the right hand or to the left.
1_24_50: Then Laban and Bethuel answered and said, 'The matter comes from the LORD; we cannot speak to you either bad or good.'
1_24_51: Here is Rebekah before you; take her and go, and let her be the wife of your master's son, as the LORD has spoken.
1_24_52: When Abraham's servant heard their words, he bowed himself to the ground before the LORD and worshiped.
1_24_53: Then the servant brought out objects of silver and gold, and clothing, and gave them to Rebekah. He also gave precious gifts to her brother and to her mother.
1_24_54: Then he and the men who were with him ate and drank and spent the night there. When they arose in the morning, he said, 'Send me away to my master.'
1_24_55: But her brother and her mother said, 'Let the young woman stay with us a few days, at least ten; after that she may go.'
1_24_56: He said to them, 'Do not delay me, since the LORD has prospered my way. Send me away that I may go to my master.'
1_24_57: They said, 'We will call the young woman and ask her opinion.'
1_24_58: So they called Rebekah and said to her, 'Will you go with this man?' She answered, 'I will go.'
1_24_59: So they sent away their sister Rebekah with her nurse, and Abraham's servant and his men.
1_24_60: And they blessed Rebekah, saying to her, 'Our sister, may you become the mother of thousands of ten thousands, and may your descendants possess the gates of those who hate them.'
1_24_61: Then Rebekah and her attendants arose, mounted the camels, and followed the man. So the servant took Rebekah and departed.
1_24_62: Now Isaac had come from Beer-lahai-roi, for he was living in the Negev region.
1_24_63: Isaac went out to meditate in the field in the early evening, and he lifted up his eyes and saw camels coming.
1_24_64: And Rebekah lifted up her eyes, and when she saw Isaac, she quickly dismounted from her camel.
1_24_65: She said to the servant, 'Who is that man walking in the field to meet us?' The servant answered, 'It is my master.' So she took a veil and covered herself.
1_24_66: And the servant told Isaac all the things that he had done.
1_24_67: Then Isaac brought her into his mother Sarah's tent, and took Rebekah as his wife. He loved her deeply, and so Isaac was comforted after his mother's death.
1_25_1: Abraham took another wife, whose name was Keturah.
1_25_2: She bore him Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
1_25_3: Jokshan fathered Sheba and Dedan. The sons of Dedan were Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
1_25_4: The sons of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the children of Keturah.
1_25_5: Abraham gave all that he had to Isaac.
1_25_6: But to the sons of his concubines Abraham gave gifts while he was still living, and sent them away from his son Isaac, eastward to the land of the east.
1_25_7: These are the days of the years of Abraham's life which he lived: one hundred and seventy-five years.
1_25_8: Then Abraham breathed his last and died in a good old age, an old man and full of years, and was gathered to his people.
1_25_9: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron the son of Zohar the Hittite, east of Mamre.
1_25_10: The field which Abraham purchased from the sons of Heth: there Abraham was buried with Sarah his wife.
1_25_11: After the death of Abraham, God blessed his son Isaac, and Isaac dwelt at Beer Lahai Roi.
1_25_12: Now these are the generations of Ishmael, Abraham's son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah's servant, bore to Abraham.
1_25_13: These are the names of the sons of Ishmael, by their names, according to their generations: the firstborn of Ishmael, Nebaioth; then Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
1_25_15: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
1_25_16: These are the sons of Ishmael and these are their names, by their villages and by their encampments, twelve princes according to their nations.
1_25_17: These are the years of the life of Ishmael: one hundred and thirty-seven years; and he breathed his last and died, and was gathered to his people.
1_25_18: They settled from Havilah to Shur, which is east of Egypt as you go toward Assyria. He settled near all his relatives.
1_25_19: Now these are the generations of Isaac, Abraham's son: Abraham fathered Isaac.
1_25_20: Isaac was forty years old when he took Rebekah as wife, the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean of Paddan-aram, the sister of Laban the Aramean.
1_25_21: Isaac prayed to the LORD on behalf of his wife, because she was barren; and the LORD answered him, and Rebekah his wife conceived.
1_25_22: But the children struggled together within her; and she said, 'If it is so, why am I like this?' So she went to inquire of the LORD.
1_25_23: And the LORD said to her: 'Two nations are in your womb, two peoples shall be separated from your body; one people shall be stronger than the other, and the older shall serve the younger.'
1_25_24: When her days to give birth were completed, behold, there were twins in her womb.
1_25_25: The first came out red, all over like a hairy garment; so they called his name Esau.
1_25_26: Afterward his brother came out with his hand grasping Esau's heel; so his name was called Jacob. Isaac was sixty years old when she bore them.
1_25_27: When the boys grew up, Esau was a skillful hunter, a man of the field; but Jacob was a peaceful man, dwelling in tents.
1_25_28: And Isaac loved Esau because he ate of his game, but Rebekah loved Jacob.
1_25_29: Once when Jacob was cooking stew, Esau came in from the field, and he was weary and famished.
1_25_30: And Esau said to Jacob, 'Please feed me with that red stew, for I am exhausted.' Therefore his name was called Edom.
1_25_31: But Jacob said, 'Sell me your birthright as of this day.'
1_25_32: And Esau said, 'Look, I am about to die; so what good is this birthright to me?'
1_25_33: Then Jacob said, 'Swear to me now.' So he swore to him, and sold his birthright to Jacob.
1_25_34: Then Jacob gave Esau bread and stew of lentils; and he ate and drank, arose, and went his way. Thus Esau despised his birthright.
1_26_1: There was a famine in the land, besides the first famine that was in the days of Abraham. And Isaac went to Abimelech king of the Philistines, in Gerar.
1_26_2: Then the LORD appeared to him and said: 'Do not go down to Egypt; live in the land of which I shall tell you.'
1_26_3: Dwell in this land, and I will be with you and bless you; for to you and your descendants I give all these lands, and I will establish the oath which I swore to Abraham your father.
1_26_4: I will multiply your descendants as the stars of heaven and will give to your descendants all these lands; and by your descendants all the nations of the earth shall be blessed;
1_26_5: because Abraham listened to me and obeyed my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws.'
1_26_7: When the men of the place asked about his wife, he said, 'She is my sister,' for he was afraid to say, 'She is my wife,' because he thought, 'the men of the place might kill me on account of Rebekah, because she is beautiful to behold.'
1_26_8: When he had been there a long time, Abimelech king of the Philistines looked out through a window and saw Isaac caressing his wife Rebekah.
1_26_9: So Abimelech called Isaac and said, 'Surely she is your wife! How could you say, "She is my sister"?' Isaac said to him, 'Because I thought I might die on account of her.'
1_26_10: Then Abimelech said, 'What is this you have done to us? One of the people might easily have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guilt upon us.'
1_26_11: So Abimelech warned all his people, saying, 'Whoever touches this man or his wife shall surely be put to death.'
1_26_12: Then Isaac sowed in that land, and reaped in the same year a hundredfold; and the LORD blessed him.
1_26_13: The man began to prosper, and continued prospering until he became very prosperous;
1_26_14: for he had possessions of flocks and herds and a great number of servants. So the Philistines envied him.
1_26_15: Now all the wells which his father's servants had dug in the days of Abraham his father, the Philistines had stopped up by filling them with earth.
1_26_16: And Abimelech said to Isaac, 'Go away from us, for you are much mightier than we.'
1_26_17: Then Isaac departed from there and pitched his tent in the Valley of Gerar, and settled there.
1_26_18: And Isaac dug again the wells of water which they had dug in the days of Abraham his father, for the Philistines had stopped them up after the death of Abraham. He gave them the same names which his father had called them.
1_26_19: And Isaac's servants dug in the valley, and found there a well of running water.
1_26_20: But the herdsmen of Gerar quarreled with Isaac's herdsmen, saying, 'The water is ours.' So he called the name of the well Esek, because they quarreled with him.
1_26_21: Then they dug another well, and they quarreled over that one also. So he called its name Sitnah.
1_26_22: And he moved from there and dug another well, and they did not quarrel over it. So he called its name Rehoboth, because he said, 'For now the LORD has made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land.'
1_26_23: Then he went up from there to Beersheba.
1_26_24: And the LORD appeared to him the same night and said, 'I am the God of your father Abraham; do not fear, for I am with you. I will bless you and multiply your descendants for my servant Abraham's sake.'
1_26_25: So he built an altar there and called on the name of the LORD, and he pitched his tent there; and there Isaac's servants dug a well.
1_26_26: Then Abimelech came to him from Gerar with Ahuzzath, one of his advisers, and Phicol the commander of his army.
1_26_27: And Isaac said to them, 'Why have you come to me, since you hate me and have sent me away from you?'
1_26_28: But they said, 'We have certainly seen that the LORD is with you. So we said, "Let there now be an oath between us, between you and us; and let us make a covenant with you,
1_26_29: that you will do us no harm, since we have not touched you, and since we have done nothing to you but good and have sent you away in peace. You are now the blessed of the LORD.'"
1_26_30: So he made them a feast, and they ate and drank.
1_26_31: Then they arose early in the morning and swore an oath with one another; and Isaac sent them away, and they departed from him in peace.
1_26_32: It came to pass the same day that Isaac's servants came and told him about the well which they had dug, and said to him, 'We have found water.'
1_26_33: So he called it Shibah. Therefore the name of the city is Beersheba to this day.
1_26_34: When Esau was forty years old, he took as wives Judith the daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Basemath the daughter of Elon the Hittite.
1_26_35: And they were a grief of mind to Isaac and Rebekah.
1_27_1: Now when Isaac was old and his eyes were so dim that he could not see, he called his older son Esau and said to him, "My son." And he answered, "Here I am."
1_27_2: He said, "Behold, I am old; I do not know the day of my death."
1_27_3: Now therefore, please take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field and hunt game for me."
1_27_4: And make me savory food, such as I love, and bring it to me that I may eat, so that my soul may bless you before I die."
1_27_5: Now Rebekah was listening when Isaac spoke to his son Esau. So when Esau went to the field to hunt for game and bring it,
1_27_6: Rebekah said to her son Jacob, "Listen, I heard your father speak to your brother Esau, saying,
1_27_7: 'Bring me game and make savory food for me, that I may eat it and bless you in the presence of the LORD before my death.'"
1_27_8: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice according to what I command you."
1_27_9: Go now to the flock and bring me two choice young goats, and I will make savory food from them for your father, such as he loves."
1_27_10: Then you shall take it to your father, that he may eat it, and that he may bless you before his death."
1_27_11: And Jacob said to Rebekah his mother, "Look, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth-skinned man."
1_27_12: Perhaps my father will feel me, and I shall seem to be a deceiver in his sight; and I shall bring a curse on myself and not a blessing."
1_27_13: But his mother said to him, "Let your curse be on me, my son; only obey my voice, and go, get them for me."
1_27_14: And he went and got them and brought them to his mother, and his mother made savory food, such as his father loved.
1_27_15: Then Rebekah took the best clothes of her elder son Esau, which were with her in the house, and put them on Jacob her younger son.
1_27_16: And she put the skins of the young goats on his hands and on the smooth part of his neck.
1_27_17: Then she gave the savory food and the bread, which she had prepared, into the hand of her son Jacob.
1_27_18: So he went to his father and said, "My father." And he said, "Here I am. Who are you, my son?"
1_27_19: Jacob said to his father, "I am Esau your firstborn; I have done just as you told me; please arise, sit and eat of my game, that your soul may bless me."
1_27_20: But Isaac said to his son, "How is it that you have found it so quickly, my son?" And he said, "Because the LORD your God brought it to me."
1_27_21: Then Isaac said to Jacob, "Please come near, that I may feel you, my son, whether you are really my son Esau or not."
1_27_22: So Jacob went near to Isaac his father, and he felt him and said, "The voice is Jacob's voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau."
1_27_23: And he did not recognize him, because his hands were hairy like his brother Esau's hands; so he blessed him.
1_27_24: Then he said, "Are you really my son Esau?" He said, "I am."
1_27_25: He said, "Bring it near to me, and I will eat of my son's game, so that my soul may bless you." So he brought it near to him, and he ate; and he brought him wine, and he drank.
1_27_26: Then his father Isaac said to him, "Come near now and kiss me, my son."
1_27_27: And he came near and kissed him; and he smelled the smell of his clothing, and blessed him and said: "Surely, the smell of my son is like the smell of a field which the LORD has blessed."
1_27_28: Therefore may God give you of the dew of heaven, of the fatness of the earth, and plenty of grain and wine."
1_27_29: Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be master over your brethren, and let your mother's sons bow down to you. Cursed be everyone who curses you, and blessed be those who bless you!"
1_27_30: Now it happened, as soon as Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, and Jacob had scarcely gone out from the presence of Isaac his father, that Esau his brother came in from his hunting.
1_27_31: He also had made savory food, and brought it to his father, and said to his father, "Let my father arise and eat of his son's game, that your soul may bless me."
1_27_32: And his father Isaac said to him, "Who are you?" So he said, "I am your son, your firstborn, Esau."
1_27_33: Then Isaac trembled violently, and said, "Who? Where is the one who hunted game and brought it to me? I ate all of it before you came, and I have blessed him—and indeed he shall be blessed."
1_27_34: When Esau heard the words of his father, he cried with an exceedingly great and bitter cry, and said to his father, "Bless me—me also, O my father!"
1_27_35: But he said, "Your brother came with deceit and has taken away your blessing."
1_27_36: And Esau said, "Is he not rightly named Jacob? For he has supplanted me these two times. He took away my birthright, and now look, he has taken away my blessing! And he said, 'Have you not reserved a blessing for me?'"
1_27_37: Then Isaac answered and said to Esau, "Indeed I have made him your master, and all his brethren I have given to him as servants; with grain and wine I have sustained him. What shall I do now for you, my son?"
1_27_38: And Esau said to his father, "Have you only one blessing, my father? Bless me—me also, O my father!" And Esau lifted up his voice and wept.
1_27_39: Then Isaac his father answered and said to him: "Behold, away from the fatness of the earth shall be your dwelling, and away from the dew of heaven from above."
1_27_40: By your sword you shall live, and you shall serve your brother; but when you become restless, you shall break his yoke from your neck."
1_27_41: So Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing with which his father blessed him, and Esau said in his heart, "The days of mourning for my father are at hand; then I will kill my brother Jacob."
1_27_42: And the words of Esau her older son were told to Rebekah. So she sent and called Jacob her younger son, and said to him, "Surely your brother Esau comforts himself concerning you by intending to kill you."
1_27_43: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice: arise, flee to my brother Laban in Haran."
1_27_44: And stay with him a few days, until your brother's fury turns away;
1_27_45: until your brother's anger turns away from you, and he forgets what you have done to him; then I will send and bring you from there. Why should I be bereaved also of you both in one day?"
1_27_46: Then Rebekah said to Isaac, "I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth; if Jacob takes a wife from the daughters of Heth, like these women of the land, what good will my life be to me?"
1_28_1: Then Isaac called Jacob and blessed him and charged him, saying: "You shall not take a wife from the daughters of Canaan."
1_28_2: Arise, go to Paddan Aram, to the house of Bethuel your mother's father, and take yourself a wife from there from the daughters of Laban your mother's brother."
1_28_3: May God Almighty bless you and make you fruitful and multiply you, so that you may become an assembly of peoples;
1_28_4: and give you the blessing of Abraham, to you and your descendants with you, that you may inherit the land of your sojournings, which God gave to Abraham."
1_28_5: Then Isaac sent Jacob away, and he went to Paddan Aram to Laban, son of Bethuel the Aramean, the brother of Rebekah, Jacob's and Esau's mother.
1_28_6: Now Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him to Paddan Aram to take a wife from there, and that as he blessed him he charged him, saying, "You shall not take a wife from the daughters of Canaan,"
1_28_7: and that Jacob had obeyed his father and mother and had gone to Paddan Aram.
1_28_8: So when Esau saw that the daughters of Canaan displeased his father Isaac,
1_28_9: he went to Ishmael and took Mahalath the daughter of Ishmael, Abraham's son, the sister of Nebaioth, to be his wife in addition to the wives he had.
1_28_10: Now Jacob left Beersheba and went toward Haran.
1_28_11: So he came to a certain place and stayed there all night, because the sun had set. Taking one of the stones of the place, he put it under his head and lay down in that place to sleep.
1_28_12: Then he dreamed, and behold, a ladder was set up on the earth with its top reaching to heaven; and there the angels of God were ascending and descending on it.
1_28_13: And behold, the LORD stood above it and said, "I am the LORD, the God of Abraham your father and the God of Isaac; the land on which you lie I will give to you and to your descendants."
1_28_14: Your descendants shall be as the dust of the earth; you shall spread abroad to the west and the east, to the north and the south; and in you and in your seed all the families of the earth shall be blessed."
1_28_15: Behold, I am with you and will keep you wherever you go, and will bring you back to this land; for I will not leave you until I have done what I have promised you."
1_28_16: Then Jacob awoke from his sleep and said, "Surely the LORD is in this place, and I did not know it!"
1_28_17: And he was afraid and said, "How awesome is this place! This is none other than the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven!"
1_28_18: Then Jacob rose early in the morning and took the stone that he had put under his head, set it up as a pillar, and poured oil on top of it.
1_28_19: And he called the name of that place Bethel; but the name of that city had been Luz previously.
1_28_20: Then Jacob made a vow, saying, "If God will be with me and will keep me in this way that I am going, and will give me bread to eat and clothing to put on,"
1_28_21: so that I come back to my father's house in peace, then the LORD shall be my God."
1_28_22: And this stone which I have set as a pillar shall be God's house, and of all that You give me I will surely give a tenth to You."
1_29_1: So Jacob went on his journey and came to the land of the people of the East.
1_29_2: He looked and saw a well in the field, and behold, three flocks of sheep lying beside it, for from that well they watered the flocks. Now the stone on the mouth of the well was large.
1_29_3: When all the flocks were gathered there, the shepherds would roll the stone from the mouth of the well and water the sheep, and put the stone back in its place over the well's mouth.
1_29_4: Jacob said to them, 'My brothers, where are you from?' And they said, 'We are from Haran.'
1_29_5: He said to them, 'Do you know Laban the son of Nahor?' They said, 'We know him.'
1_29_6: He said to them, 'Is it well with him?' They said, 'It is well; and see, Rachel his daughter is coming with the sheep.'
1_29_7: Then he said, 'Look, it is still high day; it is not time for the livestock to be gathered together. Water the sheep, and go and feed them.'
1_29_8: But they said, 'We cannot until all the flocks are gathered together, and they have rolled the stone from the well's mouth; then we water the sheep.'
1_29_9: While he was still speaking with them, Rachel came with her father's sheep, for she was a shepherdess.
1_29_10: When Jacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban his mother's brother, and the sheep of Laban his mother's brother, Jacob came near and rolled the stone from the well's mouth, and watered the flock of Laban his mother's brother.
1_29_11: Then Jacob kissed Rachel, and lifted his voice and wept.
1_29_12: And Jacob told Rachel that he was her father's relative and that he was Rebekah's son. So she ran and told her father.
1_29_13: Then it came to pass, when Laban heard the news about Jacob his sister's son, that he ran to meet him, and embraced him and kissed him, and brought him to his house. So he told Laban all these things.
1_29_14: And Laban said to him, 'Surely you are my bone and my flesh.' And he stayed with him for a month.
1_29_15: Then Laban said to Jacob, 'Because you are my relative, should you therefore serve me for nothing? Tell me, what should your wages be?'
1_29_16: Now Laban had two daughters: the name of the elder was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.
1_29_17: Leah's eyes were weak, but Rachel was beautiful in form and appearance.
1_29_18: Now Jacob loved Rachel; so he said, 'I will serve you seven years for Rachel your younger daughter.'
1_29_19: And Laban said, 'It is better that I give her to you than that I should give her to another man. Stay with me.'
1_29_20: So Jacob served seven years for Rachel, and they seemed only a few days to him because of the love he had for her.
1_29_21: Then Jacob said to Laban, 'Give me my wife, for my days are fulfilled, that I may go in to her.'
1_29_22: And Laban gathered together all the men of the place and made a feast.
1_29_23: Now it came to pass in the evening, that he took Leah his daughter and brought her to Jacob; and he went in to her.
1_29_24: And Laban gave his maid Zilpah to his daughter Leah as a maid.
1_29_25: So it came to pass in the morning, that behold, it was Leah. And he said to Laban, 'What is this you have done to me? Was it not for Rachel that I served you? Why then have you deceived me?'
1_29_26: And Laban said, 'It must not be done so in our country, to give the younger before the firstborn.'
1_29_27: Fulfill her week, and we will give you this one also for the service which you will serve with me still another seven years.'
1_29_28: Then Jacob did so and fulfilled her week. So he gave him his daughter Rachel as wife also.
1_29_29: And Laban gave his maid Bilhah to his daughter Rachel as a maid.
1_29_30: Then Jacob also went in to Rachel, and he also loved Rachel more than Leah. And he served with Laban still another seven years.
1_29_31: When the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, He opened her womb; but Rachel was barren.
1_29_32: So Leah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Reuben; for she said, 'The LORD has surely looked on my affliction. Now therefore, my husband will love me.'
1_29_33: Then she conceived again and bore a son, and said, 'Because the LORD has heard that I am unloved, He has therefore given me this son also.' And she called his name Simeon.
1_29_34: She conceived again and bore a son, and said, 'Now this time my husband will become attached to me, because I have borne him three sons.' Therefore his name was called Levi.
1_29_35: And she conceived again and bore a son, and said, 'Now I will praise the LORD.' Therefore she called his name Judah. Then she stopped bearing.
1_30_1: When Rachel saw that she was not bearing Jacob any children, she became jealous of her sister. She said to Jacob, "Give me children, or I will die!"
1_30_2: Jacob's anger burned against Rachel, and he said, "Am I in the place of God, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?"
1_30_3: She said, "Here is my maid Bilhah. Go in to her, and she will bear children on my knees, that through her I too may have children."
1_30_4: So Rachel gave him Bilhah her maid as wife, and Jacob went in to her.
1_30_5: Bilhah became pregnant and bore Jacob a son.
1_30_6: Then Rachel said, "God has judged me and has also heard my voice and given me a son." Therefore she called his name Dan.
1_30_7: Rachel's maid Bilhah conceived again and bore Jacob a second son.
1_30_8: Then Rachel said, "With mighty wrestlings I have wrestled with my sister, and I have prevailed." So she called his name Naphtali.
1_30_9: When Leah saw that she had stopped bearing children, she took her maid Zilpah and gave her to Jacob as wife.
1_30_10: Leah's maid Zilpah bore Jacob a son.
1_30_11: Then Leah said, "How fortunate!" and she called his name Gad.
1_30_12: Leah's maid Zilpah bore Jacob a second son.
1_30_13: Then Leah said, "Happy am I! For the women will call me happy," so she named him Asher.
1_30_14: In the days of wheat harvest, Reuben went and found mandrakes in the field and brought them to his mother Leah. Then Rachel said to Leah, "Please give me some of your son's mandrakes."
1_30_15: But Leah said to her, "Is it a small matter that you have taken my husband? Would you take away my son's mandrakes also?" Rachel said, "Therefore he shall lie with you tonight for your son's mandrakes."
1_30_16: When Jacob came from the field in the evening, Leah went out to meet him and said, "You must come in to me, for I have surely hired you with my son's mandrakes." And he lay with her that night.
1_30_17: God listened to Leah, and she conceived and bore Jacob a fifth son.
1_30_18: Leah said, "God has given me my wages, because I have given my maid to my husband." So she called his name Issachar.
1_30_19: Then Leah conceived again and bore Jacob a sixth son.
1_30_20: And Leah said, "God has endowed me with a good gift; now my husband will honor me, because I have borne him six sons." So she called his name Zebulun.
1_30_21: Afterward she bore a daughter, and called her name Dinah.
1_30_22: Then God remembered Rachel, and God listened to her and opened her womb.
1_30_23: She conceived and bore a son, and said, "God has taken away my reproach."
1_30_24: So she called his name Joseph, saying, "May the LORD add to me another son."
1_30_25: And when Rachel had borne Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, "Send me away, that I may go to my own place and to my country."
1_30_26: Give me my wives and my children for whom I have served you, and let me go; for you know my service which I have done for you."
1_30_27: But Laban said to him, "If I have found favor in your sight, stay, for I have learned that the LORD has blessed me because of you."
1_30_28: He said, "Name me your wages, and I will give them."
1_30_29: So Jacob said to him, "You know how I have served you, and how your livestock has fared with me."
1_30_30: For you had little before I came, but it has increased greatly, and the LORD has blessed you wherever I turned. But now, when shall I also provide for my own household?"
1_30_31: So he said, "What shall I give you?" And Jacob said, "You shall not give me anything. If you will do this thing for me, I will again feed and keep your flock."
1_30_32: Let me pass through all your flock today, removing from there all the speckled and spotted sheep, every dark-colored lamb, and the spotted and speckled among the goats; and these shall be my wages."
1_30_33: So my honesty will answer for me in time to come, when the subject of my wages comes before you: if you find any goat that is not speckled and spotted, or any lamb that is not black, they will be considered stolen."
1_30_34: And Laban said, "Good! Let it be according to your word."
1_30_35: So Laban removed that day the streaked and spotted male goats, all the speckled and spotted female goats—every one that had white in it—and all the dark-colored ones among the lambs, and gave them into the hand of his sons.
1_30_36: Then he put a three-day journey between himself and Jacob, and Jacob fed the rest of Laban's flocks.
1_30_37: Now Jacob took fresh branches of poplar, almond, and plane trees, peeled white strips in them, and exposed the white which was in the branches.
1_30_38: And he set the peeled branches before the flocks in the watering troughs where the flocks came to drink, so that they might conceive when they came to drink.
1_30_39: So the flocks mated before the branches, and the flocks brought forth streaked, speckled, and spotted young.
1_30_40: Then Jacob separated the lambs and made the flocks face toward the streaked and all the dark-colored in the flock of Laban; but he put his own flocks by themselves and did not put them with Laban's flock.
1_30_41: Whenever the stronger of the flock were mating, Jacob would place the branches in sight of the flocks in the watering troughs, that they might conceive among the branches."
1_30_42: But when the flocks were feeble, he did not put them in; so the feebler were Laban's and the stronger Jacob's.
1_30_43: Thus the man became exceedingly prosperous, and had large flocks, female and male servants, and camels and donkeys.
1_31_1: Now Jacob heard the words of Laban's sons, saying, 'Jacob has taken away all that was our father's, and from what belonged to our father he has gained all this wealth.'
1_31_2: And Jacob saw the countenance of Laban, and indeed it was not favorable toward him as before.
1_31_3: Then the LORD said to Jacob, 'Return to the land of your fathers and to your family, and I will be with you.'
1_31_4: So Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah to the field, to his flock,
1_31_5: and said to them, 'I see your father's countenance, that it is not favorable toward me as before; but the God of my father has been with me.'
1_31_6: And you know that with all my might I have served your father.
1_31_7: Yet your father has deceived me and changed my wages ten times, but God did not allow him to hurt me.
1_31_8: If he said thus: 'The speckled shall be your wages,' then all the flocks bore speckled. And if he said thus: 'The streaked shall be your wages,' then all the flocks bore streaked.
1_31_9: So God has taken away the livestock of your father and given them to me.
1_31_10: And it happened, at the time when the flocks conceived, that I lifted my eyes and saw in a dream, and behold, the rams which leaped upon the flocks were streaked, speckled, and gray-spotted.
1_31_11: Then the Angel of God spoke to me in a dream, saying, 'Jacob.' And I said, 'Here I am.'
1_31_12: And He said, 'Lift your eyes now and see, all the rams which leap on the flocks are streaked, speckled, and gray-spotted; for I have seen all that Laban is doing to you.'
1_31_13: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed the pillar and where you made a vow to Me. Now arise, get out of this land, and return to the land of your family.'
1_31_14: Then Rachel and Leah answered and said to him, 'Is there still any portion or inheritance for us in our father's house?'
1_31_15: Are we not considered strangers by him? For he has sold us, and also completely consumed our money.
1_31_16: For all these riches which God has taken from our father are really ours and our children's; now then, whatever God has said to you, do it.'
1_31_17: Then Jacob rose and set his sons and his wives on camels.
1_31_18: And he carried away all his livestock and all his possessions which he had gained, his acquired livestock which he had gained in Padan Aram, to go to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan.
1_31_19: Now Laban had gone to shear his sheep, and Rachel had stolen the household idols that were her father's.
1_31_20: And Jacob stole away, unknown to Laban the Syrian, in that he did not tell him that he intended to flee.
1_31_21: So he fled with all that he had. He arose and crossed the river, and headed toward the mountains of Gilead.
1_31_22: And Laban was told on the third day that Jacob had fled.
1_31_23: Then he took his brethren with him and pursued him for seven days' journey, and he overtook him in the mountains of Gilead.
1_31_24: But God had come to Laban the Syrian in a dream by night, and said to him, 'Be careful that you speak to Jacob neither good nor bad.'
1_31_25: So Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the mountains, and Laban with his brethren pitched in the mountains of Gilead.
1_31_26: And Laban said to Jacob: 'What have you done, that you have stolen away unknown to me, and carried away my daughters like captives taken with the sword?'
1_31_27: Why did you flee away secretly, and steal away from me, and not tell me; for I might have sent you away with joy and songs, with timbrel and harp?
1_31_28: And you did not allow me to kiss my sons and my daughters. Now you have done foolishly in so doing.
1_31_29: It is in my power to do you harm, but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, 'Be careful that you speak to Jacob neither good nor bad.'
1_31_30: And now you have surely gone because you greatly long for your father's house, but why did you steal my gods?'
1_31_31: Then Jacob answered and said to Laban, 'Because I was afraid, for I said, "Perhaps you would take your daughters from me by force."'
1_31_32: With whomever you find your gods, do not let him live. In the presence of our brethren, identify what I have of yours and take it with you.' For Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.
1_31_33: And Laban went into Jacob's tent, into Leah's tent, and into the two maids' tents, but he did not find them. Then he went out of Leah's tent and entered Rachel's tent.
1_31_34: Now Rachel had taken the household idols, put them in the camel's saddle, and sat on them. And Laban searched all about the tent but did not find them.
1_31_35: And she said to her father, 'Let it not displease my lord that I cannot rise before you, for the manner of women is with me.' And he searched but did not find the household idols.
1_31_36: Then Jacob was angry and rebuked Laban, and Jacob answered and said to Laban: 'What is my trespass? What is my sin, that you have so hotly pursued me?'
1_31_37: Although you have searched all my things, what part of your household things have you found? Set it here before my brethren and your brethren, that they may judge between us both!
1_31_38: These twenty years I have been with you; your ewes and your female goats have not miscarried their young, and I have not eaten the rams of your flock.
1_31_39: That which was torn by beasts I did not bring to you; I bore the loss of it. You required it from my hand, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.
1_31_40: There I was! In the day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night, and my sleep departed from my eyes.
1_31_41: Thus I have been in your house twenty years; I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you have changed my wages ten times.
1_31_42: Unless the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had been with me, surely now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God has seen my affliction and the labor of my hands, and rebuked you last night.'
1_31_43: And Laban answered and said to Jacob, 'These daughters are my daughters, and these children are my children, and this flock is my flock; all that you see is mine. But what can I do this day to these my daughters or to their children whom they have borne?'
1_31_44: Now therefore, come, let us make a covenant, you and I, and let it be a witness between you and me.'
1_31_45: So Jacob took a stone and set it up as a pillar.
1_31_46: Then Jacob said to his relatives, 'Gather stones.' So they took stones and made a heap, and they ate there beside the heap.
1_31_47: Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha (which means 'witness pile' in Aramaic), but Jacob called it Galeed (which means 'witness pile' in Hebrew).
1_31_48: And Laban said, 'This heap is a witness between you and me today.' Therefore it was named Galeed, for the place was called a Witness Pile.
1_31_49: And also Mizpah (which means 'watchtower'), for he said, 'May the LORD watch between you and me when we are absent one from another.'
1_31_50: If you mistreat my daughters or if you take other wives besides my daughters, although no one is with us, see, God is witness between you and me.'
1_31_51: Then Laban said to Jacob, 'Behold this heap and behold this pillar which I have set between you and me.'
1_31_52: This heap is a witness, and this pillar is a witness, that I will not pass beyond this heap to you, and you will not pass beyond this heap and this pillar to me, for harm.
1_31_53: The God of Abraham and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge between us.' So Jacob swore by the Fear of his father Isaac.
1_31_54: Then Jacob offered a sacrifice on the mountain and called his kinsmen to eat bread. They ate bread and spent the night on the mountain.
1_31_55: Early in the morning Laban arose and kissed his grandchildren and his daughters and blessed them. Then Laban departed and returned to his place.
1_32_1: So Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him to reassure and protect him.
1_32_2: When Jacob saw them, he said, 'This is God's camp!' So he called the name of that place Mahanaim.
1_32_3: Then Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to his brother Esau in the land of Seir, the country of Edom.
1_32_4: He commanded them, saying, 'Thus shall you speak to my lord Esau: Your servant Jacob says, I have been staying with Laban and have been delayed until now.'
1_32_5: I have oxen, donkeys, flocks, and male and female servants; and I have sent to tell my lord, that I may find favor in your sight.'"
1_32_6: When the messengers returned to Jacob, they said, 'We came to your brother Esau, and he is coming to meet you — and he has four hundred men with him.'
1_32_7: Jacob was greatly afraid and distressed; he divided the people with him, and the flocks and herds and camels, into two camps.
1_32_8: He thought, 'If Esau comes to the one camp and attacks it, then the other camp that is left will escape.'
1_32_9: Then Jacob said, 'O God of my father Abraham and God of my father Isaac, O LORD who said to me, "Return to your country and to your relatives, and I will prosper you"'
1_32_10: I am unworthy of all the lovingkindness and faithfulness you have shown to your servant; for with only my staff I crossed this Jordan, and now I have become two camps.
1_32_11: Please deliver me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau; for I fear him, lest he come and attack me and the mothers with the children.
1_32_12: For You said, 'I will surely prosper you and make your descendants as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.'"
1_32_13: So he lodged there that night and selected from what he had a present for his brother Esau:
1_32_14: two hundred female goats and twenty male goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams,
1_32_15: thirty milk camels with their young, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty female donkeys and ten male donkeys.
1_32_16: He entrusted them to his servants as separate herds and said to them, 'Go on ahead of me and keep a space between the herds.'
1_32_17: And he instructed the first one: 'When my brother Esau meets you and asks you, "To whom do you belong? Where are you going? And whose are these ahead of you?"'
1_32_18: then you shall say, 'They belong to your servant Jacob; they are a gift sent to my lord Esau. And look, he is coming behind us.'"
1_32_19: So he commanded the second, the third, and all who followed the herds, saying, 'In this manner you shall speak to Esau when you find him;'
1_32_20: and also say, 'Behold, your servant Jacob is behind us.'" For he thought, 'I will appease him with the gift that goes before me, and afterward I will see his face; perhaps he will accept me.'
1_32_21: So the present went on ahead of him, while he himself stayed that night in the camp.
1_32_22: During the night Jacob got up and took his two wives, his two servant wives, and his eleven sons, and crossed over the ford of the Jabbok.
1_32_23: He took them and sent them across the stream, along with all his possessions.
1_32_24: Then Jacob was left alone, and a Man wrestled with him until daybreak.
1_32_25: When the Man saw that He could not defeat him, He touched the socket of Jacob's hip, and Jacob's hip was dislocated as he wrestled with Him.
1_32_26: Then He said, 'Let Me go, for the day is breaking.' But Jacob said, 'I will not let You go unless You bless me.'
1_32_27: So He said to him, 'What is your name?' And he said, 'Jacob.'
1_32_28: And He said, 'Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel; for you have struggled with God and with men, and have prevailed.'
1_32_29: Then Jacob asked Him, 'Please tell me Your name.' But He said, 'Why is it that you ask My name?' And He blessed him there.
1_32_30: So Jacob called the name of the place Peniel: 'For I have seen God face to face, and yet my life has been preserved.'
1_32_31: The sun rose upon him as he passed over Penuel, limping because of his hip.
1_32_32: Therefore to this day the people of Israel do not eat the sinew of the hip, because He touched the socket of Jacob's thigh in the sinew of the hip.
1_33_1: Now Jacob lifted his eyes and looked, and there was Esau coming with four hundred men. So he divided the children among Leah, Rachel, and the two maidservants.
1_33_2: He put the maidservants and their children in front, Leah and her children next, and Rachel and Joseph last.
1_33_3: Then he himself went on ahead of them and bowed himself to the ground seven times until he came near to his brother.
1_33_4: But Esau ran to meet him and embraced him, fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept.
1_33_5: Then Esau lifted his eyes and saw the women and children, and said, 'Who are these with you?' Jacob answered, 'The children whom God has graciously given your servant.'
1_33_6: Then the maidservants came near with their children, and they bowed down.
1_33_7: Leah also came near with her children, and they bowed down; and afterward Joseph and Rachel came near, and they bowed down.
1_33_8: Then Esau said, 'What do you mean by all this company which I met?' And Jacob said, 'These are to find favor in the sight of my lord.'
1_33_9: But Esau said, 'I have enough, my brother; keep what you have for yourself.'
1_33_10: And Jacob said, 'No, please, if I have now found favor in your sight, then receive my present from my hand, inasmuch as I have seen your face as though I had seen the face of God, and you have received me favorably.'
1_33_11: Please take my blessing that is brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me and I have enough.' So he urged him, and he took it.
1_33_12: Then Esau said, 'Let us take our journey; let us go, and I will go before you.'
1_33_13: But Jacob said to him, 'My lord knows that the children are weak and the flocks and herds with nursing young are with me. And if they are driven hard one day, all the flock will die.'
1_33_14: Please let my lord go on ahead before his servant. I will lead on slowly at a pace which the livestock that go before me and the children are able to endure, until I come to my lord in Seir.'
1_33_15: And Esau said, 'Now let me leave with you some of the people who are with me.' But he said, 'What need is there? Let me find favor in the sight of my lord.'
1_33_16: So Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.
1_33_17: And Jacob journeyed to Succoth, built himself a house, and made booths for his livestock. Therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.
1_33_18: Then Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, when he came from Paddan-aram, and he pitched his tent before the city.
1_33_19: And he bought the parcel of land where he had pitched his tent from the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, for one hundred pieces of money.
1_33_20: Then he erected an altar there and called it El-Elohe-Israel.
1_34_1: Now Dinah, the daughter of Leah whom she had borne to Jacob, went out to visit the young women of the land.
1_34_2: When Shechem, the son of Hamor the Hivite, the prince of the land, saw her, he took her and lay with her by force, humiliating and defiling her.
1_34_3: His soul was deeply drawn to Dinah, the daughter of Jacob; he loved the young woman and spoke tenderly to her heart.
1_34_4: So Shechem spoke to his father Hamor, saying, 'Get me this young woman as a wife.'
1_34_5: Now Jacob heard that Shechem had defiled his daughter Dinah; but since his sons were with his livestock in the field, Jacob held his peace until they came in.
1_34_6: Then Hamor, the father of Shechem, went out to Jacob to speak with him.
1_34_7: Jacob's sons came in from the field when they heard of it, and the men were deeply grieved and very angry, because Shechem had done an outrageous thing in Israel by lying with Jacob's daughter, a thing that ought not to be done.
1_34_8: But Hamor spoke with them, saying, 'The soul of my son Shechem longs for your daughter. Please give her to him as a wife.'
1_34_9: Intermarry with us; give your daughters to us, and take our daughters for yourselves.
1_34_10: So you shall dwell with us, and the land shall be open before you. Settle here, move about, and acquire property in it.
1_34_11: Then Shechem said to her father and brothers, 'Let me find favor in your eyes, and whatever you say to me I will give.'
1_34_12: Ask me ever so much dowry and gift, and I will give according to what you say to me; only give me the young woman as a wife.
1_34_13: But Jacob's sons answered Shechem and his father Hamor deceitfully, because he had defiled their sister Dinah.
1_34_14: They said to them, 'We cannot do this thing, to give our sister to one who is uncircumcised, for that would be a disgrace to us.'
1_34_15: But only on this condition will we consent to you: if you will become as we are, in that every male among you is circumcised.
1_34_16: Then we will give our daughters to you, and we will take your daughters for ourselves, and we will dwell with you and become one people.
1_34_17: But if you will not listen to us and be circumcised, then we will take our daughter and be gone.
1_34_18: Their words seemed reasonable to Hamor and his son Shechem.
1_34_19: The young man did not delay to do the thing, because he was delighted with Jacob's daughter. Now he was the most honored of all his father's household.
1_34_20: So Hamor and his son Shechem went to the gate of their city and spoke with the men of their city.
1_34_21: 'These men are peaceful toward us; therefore let them dwell in the land and trade in it, for the land is large enough for them. Let us take their daughters as wives and give our daughters to them.'
1_34_22: 'Only on this condition will the men consent to live with us and become one people: that every male among us be circumcised as they are circumcised.'
1_34_23: 'Will not their livestock and their property and all their animals be ours? Only let us consent to them, and they will dwell with us.'
1_34_24: All the men who went out of the gate of his city listened to Hamor and his son Shechem, and every male was circumcised.
1_34_25: On the third day, when they were still in pain, two of Jacob's sons, Simeon and Levi, Dinah's brothers, each took his sword and came upon the unsuspecting city and killed all the males.
1_34_26: They killed Hamor and his son Shechem with the sword, and took Dinah from Shechem's house and went away.
1_34_27: Jacob's sons came upon the slain and plundered the city because their sister had been defiled.
1_34_28: They took their flocks and their herds, their donkeys, and everything that was in the city and in the field.
1_34_29: All their wealth and all their little ones and their wives they captured and looted, and they plundered everything in the houses.
1_34_30: Then Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, 'You have brought trouble on me, making me odious among the inhabitants of the land, among the Canaanites and the Perizzites. My numbers are few, and if they gather themselves together against me and attack me, I shall be destroyed, both I and my household.'
1_34_31: But they said, 'Should he treat our sister like a prostitute?'
1_35_1: Then God said to Jacob, 'Arise, go up to Bethel and dwell there; make an altar there to God, who appeared to you when you fled from the face of Esau your brother.'
1_35_2: So Jacob said to his household and to all who were with him, 'Put away the foreign gods that are among you, purify yourselves, and change your garments.'
1_35_3: Then let us arise and go up to Bethel, and I will make an altar there to God, who answered me in the day of my distress and has been with me wherever I have gone.'
1_35_4: So they gave Jacob all the foreign gods which were in their hands and the rings which were in their ears; and Jacob hid them under the oak tree which was near Shechem.
1_35_5: As they journeyed, a terror from God fell upon the cities that were all around them, so that they did not pursue the sons of Jacob.
1_35_6: So Jacob came to Luz (that is, Bethel), which is in the land of Canaan, he and all the people who were with him.
1_35_7: There he built an altar and called the place El-bethel, because God had revealed Himself to him when he fled from his brother.
1_35_8: Now Deborah, Rebekah's nurse, died and was buried below Bethel under the oak tree. So Jacob named the place Allon-bacuth.
1_35_9: Then God appeared to Jacob again when he came from Paddan-aram, and blessed him.
1_35_10: God said to him, 'Your name is Jacob; you shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel shall be your name.' So He called his name Israel.
1_35_11: God also said to him, 'I am God Almighty. Be fruitful and multiply; a nation and a company of nations shall come from you, and kings shall come from your body.'
1_35_12: The land which I gave to Abraham and Isaac I will give it to you, and I will give the land to your descendants after you.'
1_35_13: Then God went up from him in the place where He had spoken with him.
1_35_14: Jacob set up a pillar in the place where He had spoken with him, a pillar of stone; and he poured a drink offering on it and poured oil on it.
1_35_15: So Jacob called the name of the place where God had spoken with him Bethel.
1_35_16: Then they journeyed from Bethel. When they were still some distance from Ephrath, Rachel went into labor, and she had hard labor.
1_35_17: When she was in hard labor, the midwife said to her, 'Do not fear; you will have this son also.'
1_35_18: As her soul was departing (for she died), she called his name Ben-oni; but his father called him Benjamin.
1_35_19: So Rachel died and was buried on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem).
1_35_20: Jacob set up a pillar on her grave; it is the pillar of Rachel's grave to this day.
1_35_21: Then Israel journeyed on and pitched his tent beyond the tower of Eder.
1_35_22: While Israel dwelt in that land, Reuben went and lay with Bilhah his father's concubine; and Israel heard about it. Now the sons of Jacob were twelve:
1_35_23: The sons of Leah were Reuben, Jacob's firstborn, and Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun;
1_35_24: The sons of Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin;
1_35_25: The sons of Bilhah, Rachel's maidservant, were Dan and Naphtali;
1_35_26: And the sons of Zilpah, Leah's maidservant, were Gad and Asher. These were the sons of Jacob who were born to him in Paddan-aram.
1_35_27: Then Jacob came to his father Isaac at Mamre, or Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac had dwelt.
1_35_28: Now the days of Isaac were one hundred and eighty years.
1_35_29: So Isaac breathed his last and died, and was gathered to his people, being old and full of days. And his sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
1_36_1: Now this is the genealogy of Esau, who is Edom—the account of the generations of his descendants.
1_36_2: Esau took his wives from the daughters of Canaan: Adah the daughter of Elon the Hittite; Oholibamah the daughter of Anah, the granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite;
1_36_3: and Basemath, Ishmael's daughter, sister of Nebaioth.
1_36_4: Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau, and Basemath bore Reuel.
1_36_5: And Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah. These were the sons of Esau who were born to him in the land of Canaan.
1_36_6: Then Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, and all the members of his household, his livestock and all his cattle and all his goods which he had acquired in the land of Canaan, and went to a land away from his brother Jacob.
1_36_7: For their possessions were too great for them to dwell together, and the land where they sojourned could not sustain them because of their livestock.
1_36_8: So Esau settled in the hill country of Seir. Esau is Edom.
1_36_9: These are the generations of Esau the father of the Edomites in the hill country of Seir.
1_36_10: These were the names of Esau's sons: Eliphaz the son of Adah, Esau's wife, and Reuel the son of Basemath, Esau's wife.
1_36_11: The sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.
1_36_12: Now Timna was the concubine of Eliphaz, Esau's son, and she bore Amalek to Eliphaz. These were the sons of Adah, Esau's wife.
1_36_13: These were the sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. These were the sons of Basemath, Esau's wife.
1_36_14: These were the sons of Esau's wife Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and granddaughter of Zibeon: she bore to Esau Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
1_36_15: These were the chiefs of the sons of Esau. The sons of Eliphaz the firstborn of Esau were Chief Teman, Chief Omar, Chief Zepho, Chief Kenaz,
1_36_16: Chief Korah, Chief Gatam, and Chief Amalek. These were the chiefs descended from Eliphaz in the land of Edom; they were the sons of Adah.
1_36_17: These were the sons of Reuel, Esau's son: Chief Nahath, Chief Zerah, Chief Shammah, and Chief Mizzah. These were the chiefs descended from Reuel in the land of Edom; they were the sons of Esau's wife Basemath.
1_36_18: These were the sons of Esau's wife Oholibamah: Chief Jeush, Chief Jalam, and Chief Korah. These were the chiefs descended from Esau's wife Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah.
1_36_19: These were the sons of Esau (that is, Edom), and these were their chiefs.
1_36_20: These are the sons of Seir the Horite, the inhabitants of the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
1_36_21: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. These were the chiefs of the Horites, the sons of Seir in the land of Edom.
1_36_22: The sons of Lotan were Hori and Hemam; and Lotan's sister was Timna.
1_36_23: These are the sons of Shobal: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
1_36_24: These are the sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah. This was the Anah who found the hot springs in the wilderness as he pastured the donkeys of his father Zibeon.
1_36_25: These were the children of Anah: Dishon and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah.
1_36_26: These are the sons of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
1_36_27: These are the sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
1_36_28: These are the sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.
1_36_29: These were the chiefs of the Horites: Chief Lotan, Chief Shobal, Chief Zibeon, Chief Anah,
1_36_30: Chief Dishon, Chief Ezer, and Chief Dishan. These were the chiefs of the Horites, according to their chiefs in the land of Seir.
1_36_31: Now these were the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before any king reigned over the children of Israel:
1_36_32: Bela the son of Beor reigned in Edom, and the name of his city was Dinhabah.
1_36_33: When Bela died, Jobab the son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned in his place.
1_36_34: When Jobab died, Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.
1_36_35: When Husham died, Hadad the son of Bedad, who defeated Midian in the field of Moab, reigned in his place; and the name of his city was Avith.
1_36_36: When Hadad died, Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his place.
1_36_37: When Samlah died, Shaul of Rehoboth on the River reigned in his place.
1_36_38: When Shaul died, Baal-hanan the son of Achbor reigned in his place.
1_36_39: When Baal-hanan the son of Achbor died, Hadar reigned in his place; the name of his city was Pau, and his wife's name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred, the daughter of Mezahab.
1_36_40: These were the names of the chiefs of Esau, according to their families and their dwelling places, by their names: Chief Timna, Chief Alvah, Chief Jetheth,
1_36_41: Chief Oholibamah, Chief Elah, Chief Pinon,
1_36_42: Chief Kenaz, Chief Teman, Chief Mibzar,
1_36_43: Chief Magdiel, and Chief Iram. These were the chiefs of Edom, according to their settlements in the land they possessed. Esau was the father of the Edomites.
1_37_1: Now Jacob lived in the land where his father had been a stranger and sojourned, in the land of Canaan.
1_37_2: These are the generations of Jacob. Joseph, being seventeen years old, was pasturing the flock with his brothers. He was a young man with the sons of Bilhah and Zilpah, his father's wives; and Joseph brought a bad report of them to his father.
1_37_3: Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his sons, because he was the son of his old age. And he made him a robe of many colors.
1_37_4: But when his brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, they hated him and could not speak peaceably to him.
1_37_5: Now Joseph had a dream, and when he told it to his brothers they hated him even more.
1_37_6: He said to them, 'Please hear this dream that I have dreamed:'
1_37_7: Behold, we were binding sheaves in the field. Then my sheaf arose and stood upright; and indeed your sheaves stood all around and bowed down to my sheaf.
1_37_8: His brothers said to him, 'Are you indeed to reign over us? Or are you really going to rule over us?' So they hated him even more for his dreams and for his words.
1_37_9: Then he dreamed another dream and told it to his brothers, saying, 'Look, I have dreamed another dream. Behold, the sun, the moon, and eleven stars were bowing down to me.'
1_37_10: But when he told it to his father and to his brothers, his father rebuked him and said to him, 'What is this dream that you have dreamed? Shall I and your mother and your brothers indeed come to bow ourselves down before you to the ground?'
1_37_11: And his brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept the saying in mind.
1_37_12: Then his brothers went to pasture their father's flock in Shechem.
1_37_13: And Israel said to Joseph, 'Are not your brothers pasturing the flock in Shechem? Come, I will send you to them.' So he said to him, 'Here I am.'
1_37_14: Then he said to him, 'Please go and see if it is well with your brothers and well with the flocks, and bring back word to me.' So he sent him from the Valley of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.
1_37_15: Now a man found him wandering in the fields. And the man asked him, 'What are you seeking?'
1_37_16: So he said, 'I am seeking my brothers; please tell me where they are pasturing their flocks.'
1_37_17: And the man said, 'They have departed from here, for I heard them say, "Let us go to Dothan."' So Joseph went after his brothers and found them in Dothan.
1_37_18: Now when they saw him from afar off, even before he came near them, they conspired against him to kill him.
1_37_19: Then they said to one another, 'Look, this dreamer is coming!'
1_37_20: 'Come now therefore, and let us kill him and cast him into some pit; and we shall say, "A wild beast has devoured him." We shall see what will become of his dreams!'
1_37_21: But Reuben heard it, and he delivered him out of their hands, saying, 'Let us not kill him.'
1_37_22: And Reuben said to them, 'Shed no blood, but cast him into this pit that is in the wilderness, and lay no hand on him'—that he might deliver him out of their hands to restore him to his father.
1_37_23: So it came to pass, when Joseph had come to his brothers, that they stripped Joseph of his tunic, the coat of many colors that was on him.
1_37_24: And they took him and threw him into the pit. Now the pit was empty; there was no water in it.
1_37_25: Then they sat down to eat a meal. And lifting up their eyes they looked, and behold, a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, with their camels bearing spices, balm, and myrrh, going on their way to carry them down to Egypt.
1_37_26: So Judah said to his brothers, 'What profit is there if we kill our brother and conceal his blood?'
1_37_27: 'Come and let us sell him to the Ishmaelites, and let not our hand be upon him, for he is our brother and our flesh.' And his brothers listened to him.
1_37_28: Then Midianite merchants passed by; so the brothers pulled Joseph up and lifted him out of the pit, and sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty shekels of silver. And they took Joseph to Egypt.
1_37_29: Now Reuben returned to the pit, and behold, Joseph was not in the pit; so he tore his clothes.
1_37_30: And he returned to his brothers and said, 'The boy is gone; and I, where shall I go?'
1_37_31: So they took Joseph's tunic, slaughtered a male goat, and dipped the tunic in the blood.
1_37_32: Then they sent the coat of many colors and brought it to their father and said, 'We have found this. Please examine whether it is your son's coat or not.'
1_37_33: And he recognized it and said, 'It is my son's coat. A wild beast has devoured him. Joseph is without doubt torn to pieces!'
1_37_34: Then Jacob tore his clothes, put sackcloth on his waist, and mourned for his son many days.
1_37_35: And all his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him; but he refused to be comforted, and he said, 'Surely I will go down to Sheol to my son, mourning.' Thus his father wept for him.
1_37_36: Now the Midianites had sold him in Egypt to Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh and captain of the guard.
1_38_1: At that time Judah departed from his brothers and visited a certain Adullamite whose name was Hirah, settling near him.
1_38_2: There Judah saw a daughter of a certain Canaanite whose name was Shua; he took her as his wife and went in to her.
1_38_3: So she conceived and bore a son, and he named him Er.
1_38_4: She conceived again and bore a son, and she named him Onan.
1_38_5: She conceived yet again and bore a son, and named him Shelah; it was at Chezib when she gave birth to him.
1_38_6: Then Judah took a wife for Er his firstborn, and her name was Tamar.
1_38_7: But Er, Judah's firstborn, was wicked in the sight of the LORD, and the LORD put him to death.
1_38_8: Then Judah said to Onan, 'Go in to your brother's wife and perform the duty of a brother-in-law to her, and raise up offspring for your brother.'
1_38_9: But Onan knew that the offspring would not be his; so whenever he went in to his brother's wife, he spilled his semen on the ground in order not to give children to his brother.
1_38_10: What he did was displeasing in the sight of the LORD, so He took his life also.
1_38_11: Then Judah said to Tamar his daughter-in-law, 'Remain a widow in your father's house until my son Shelah grows up,' for he feared that he would die, like his brothers. So Tamar went and remained in her father's house.
1_38_12: After considerable time, the wife of Judah, Shua's daughter, died; and when the time of mourning was over, Judah went up to Timnah to his sheepshearers, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite.
1_38_13: Tamar was told, 'Behold, your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep.'
1_38_14: So she removed her widow's garments, covered herself with a veil, wrapped herself, and sat at the entrance to Enaim, which is on the road to Timnah; for she saw that Shelah had grown up, and she had not been given to him as a wife.
1_38_15: When Judah saw her, he thought she was a prostitute, for she had covered her face.
1_38_16: He turned to her by the roadside and said, 'Come, let me sleep with you,' for he did not know that she was his daughter-in-law. She said, 'What will you give me, that you may come in to me?'
1_38_17: He answered, 'I will send you a young goat from the flock.' She said, 'Only if you give me a pledge until you send it.'
1_38_18: He said, 'What pledge shall I give you?' She replied, 'Your seal, your cord, and your staff that is in your hand.' So he gave them to her and went in to her, and she conceived by him.
1_38_19: Then she got up and left, removed her veil, and put on her widow's garments.
1_38_20: When Judah sent the young goat by his friend the Adullamite to take back the pledge from the woman's hand, he could not find her.
1_38_21: He asked the men of the place, 'Where is the shrine prostitute who was at Enaim by the roadside?' And they said, 'There has been no prostitute here.'
1_38_22: So he returned to Judah and said, 'I have not found her; and moreover, the men of the place said, there has been no prostitute here.'
1_38_23: Then Judah said, 'Let her keep the items for herself, lest we be shamed. After all, I sent this young goat, but you could not find her.'
1_38_24: About three months later Judah was told, 'Tamar your daughter-in-law has been acting like a prostitute, and now she is pregnant.' Judah said, 'Bring her out, and let her be burned!'
1_38_25: As she was being brought out, she sent word to her father-in-law, 'I am pregnant by the man to whom these belong. Please examine them. Whose seal, cord, and staff are these?'
1_38_26: Then Judah recognized them and said, 'She is more righteous than I, since I did not give her to my son Shelah.' And he did not know her again.
1_38_27: When the time came for her to give birth, there were twins in her womb.
1_38_28: And when she was in labor, one put out his hand; and the midwife took and tied a scarlet thread on his hand, saying, 'This one came out first.'
1_38_29: But as he drew back his hand, his brother came out. And she said, 'What a breakout you have made for yourself!' So he was named Perez.
1_38_30: Afterward his brother came out with the scarlet thread on his hand, and he was named Zerah.
1_39_1: Now Joseph had been taken down to Egypt. Potiphar, an Egyptian officer of Pharaoh and captain of the guard, bought him from the Ishmaelites who had taken him down there.
1_39_2: The LORD was with Joseph, and he became a successful man, serving in the household of his Egyptian master.
1_39_3: His master saw that the LORD was with him and that the LORD caused all that he did to prosper in his hands.
1_39_4: So Joseph found favor in his sight and became his personal attendant. Potiphar made him overseer of his house, and all that he owned he put under Joseph's authority.
1_39_5: From the time that he made him overseer of his house and all that he owned, the LORD blessed the Egyptian's house for Joseph's sake; the blessing of the LORD was on all that he had, in house and field.
1_39_6: So he left all that he had in Joseph's charge, and because of him he had no concern about anything except the food which he ate. Now Joseph was handsome in form and appearance.
1_39_7: After some time his master's wife cast longing eyes on Joseph and said, 'Lie with me.'
1_39_8: But he refused and said to his master's wife, 'Look, with me here, my master does not concern himself with anything in the house, and he has put all that he owns in my charge.'
1_39_9: There is no one greater in this house than I, nor has he kept back anything from me but you, because you are his wife. How then can I do this great wickedness and sin against God?'
1_39_10: Although she spoke to Joseph day after day, he refused to lie beside her or to be with her.
1_39_11: Now one day he went into the house to do his work, and none of the men of the household was there inside.
1_39_12: She caught him by his garment, saying, 'Lie with me!' But he left his garment in her hand and fled and ran outside.
1_39_13: When she saw that he had left his garment in her hand and had fled outside,
1_39_14: She called to the men of her house and spoke to them, saying, 'See, he has brought in a Hebrew to mock us. He came in to me to lie with me, and I cried out with a loud voice.'
1_39_15: When he heard that I lifted my voice and cried out, he left his garment with me and fled and went outside.'
1_39_16: So she kept his garment with her until his master came home.
1_39_17: Then she spoke to him with these words, saying, 'The Hebrew servant whom you brought to us came in to mock me;'
1_39_18: So it happened, as I lifted my voice and cried out, that he left his garment with me and fled outside.'
1_39_19: When his master heard the words which his wife spoke to him, saying, 'Your servant did to me after this manner,' his anger burned.
1_39_20: Then Joseph's master took him and put him into the prison, the place where the king's prisoners were confined. And he was there in the prison.
1_39_21: But the LORD was with Joseph and showed him mercy, and He gave him favor in the sight of the keeper of the prison.
1_39_22: And the keeper of the prison committed to Joseph's hand all the prisoners who were in the prison; whatever they did there, it was his doing.
1_39_23: The keeper of the prison did not look into anything that was under Joseph's authority, because the LORD was with him; and whatever he did, the LORD made it prosper.
1_40_1: After these things, the butler and the baker of the king of Egypt offended their lord, the king of Egypt.
1_40_2: And Pharaoh was angry with his two officers, the chief butler and the chief baker.
1_40_3: So he put them in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, in the prison where Joseph was confined.
1_40_4: The captain of the guard charged Joseph with them, and he served them; they continued in custody for some time.
1_40_5: Then the butler and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were confined in the prison, each had a dream in one night, and each dream had its own meaning.
1_40_6: When Joseph came to them in the morning, he looked at them and saw that they were sad.
1_40_7: So he asked Pharaoh's officers who were with him in custody in his lord's house, saying, 'Why do you look so sad today?'
1_40_8: And they said to him, 'We have each had a dream, and there is no one to interpret it.' So Joseph said to them, 'Do not interpretations belong to God? Tell them to me, please.'
1_40_9: Then the chief butler told his dream to Joseph and said to him, 'Behold, in my dream a vine was before me,'
1_40_10: and on the vine were three branches; as soon as it budded, its blossoms shot forth, and its clusters produced ripe grapes.
1_40_11: Now Pharaoh's cup was in my hand; so I took the grapes and pressed them into Pharaoh's cup, and placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.'
1_40_12: And Joseph said to him, 'This is the interpretation of it: The three branches are three days.'
1_40_13: Within three days Pharaoh will lift up your head and restore you to your place, and you will put Pharaoh's cup in his hand according to the former manner, when you were his butler.
1_40_14: But remember me when it is well with you, and please show kindness to me; make mention of me to Pharaoh, and get me out of this house.
1_40_15: For indeed I was stolen away from the land of the Hebrews; and also I have done nothing here that they should put me into the dungeon.'
1_40_16: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Joseph, 'I also had a dream: there were three white baskets on my head,'
1_40_17: and in the uppermost basket were all sorts of baked foods for Pharaoh, but the birds were eating them out of the basket on my head.'
1_40_18: So Joseph answered and said, 'This is the interpretation of it: The three baskets are three days.'
1_40_19: Within three days Pharaoh will lift off your head from you and hang you on a tree; and the birds will eat your flesh from you.'
1_40_20: Now it came to pass on the third day, which was Pharaoh's birthday, that he made a feast for all his servants; and he lifted up the head of the chief butler and of the chief baker among his servants.
1_40_21: Then he restored the chief butler to his position, and he placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.
1_40_22: But he hanged the chief baker, as Joseph had interpreted to them.
1_40_23: Yet the chief butler did not remember Joseph, but forgot him.
1_41_1: Then at the end of two full years, Pharaoh had a dream; and behold, he stood by the Nile River.
1_41_2: Suddenly there came up out of the river seven cows, fine looking and fat, sleek and handsome; and they fed in the meadow and marsh grass.
1_41_3: Then behold, seven other cows came up after them out of the river, ugly and gaunt, thin and blighted, and stood by the other cows on the bank of the river.
1_41_4: And the ugly and gaunt cows ate up the seven fine looking and fat cows. So Pharaoh awoke.
1_41_5: He slept and dreamed a second time; and suddenly seven heads of grain came up on one stalk, plump and good.
1_41_6: Then behold, seven thin heads, blighted and scorched by the east wind, sprang up after them.
1_41_7: And the seven thin heads devoured the seven plump and full heads. So Pharaoh awoke, and indeed, it was a dream.
1_41_8: Now in the morning his spirit was troubled, and he sent and called for all the magicians of Egypt and all its wise men. And Pharaoh told them his dreams, but there was no one who could interpret them for Pharaoh.
1_41_9: Then the chief cupbearer spoke to Pharaoh, saying: "I remember my faults this day."
1_41_10: When Pharaoh was angry with his servants, and put me in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, both me and the chief baker,
1_41_11: we each had a dream in one night, he and I. Each of us dreamed according to the interpretation of his own dream.
1_41_12: Now there was a young Hebrew man with us there, a servant of the captain of the guard. And we told him, and he interpreted our dreams for us; to each man he interpreted according to his own dream.
1_41_13: And it came to pass, just as he interpreted for us, so it happened. He restored me to my office, and he hanged him."
1_41_14: Then Pharaoh sent and called Joseph, and they brought him quickly out of the dungeon; and when he had shaved and changed his clothing, he came to Pharaoh.
1_41_15: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, "I have had a dream, and there is no one who can interpret it. But I have heard it said of you that when you hear a dream you can interpret it."
1_41_16: So Joseph answered Pharaoh, saying, "It is not in me; God will give Pharaoh a favorable answer."
