1_41_17: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph: "Behold, in my dream I stood on the bank of the Nile River."
1_41_18: Suddenly seven cows came up out of the river, fine looking and fat, sleek and handsome; and they fed in the meadow and marsh grass.
1_41_19: Then behold, seven other cows came up after them, poor and very ugly and gaunt, such ugliness as I have never seen in all the land of Egypt.
1_41_20: And the gaunt and ugly cows ate up the first seven, the fat cows.
1_41_21: When they had eaten them up, no one would have known that they had eaten them, for they were still as ugly as at the beginning. So I awoke.
1_41_22: Also I saw in my dream, and suddenly seven heads of grain came up on one stalk, full and good.
1_41_23: Then behold, seven heads, withered, thin, and blighted by the east wind, sprang up after them.
1_41_24: And the thin heads devoured the seven good heads. So I told this to the magicians, but there was no one who could explain it to me."
1_41_25: Then Joseph said to Pharaoh, "The dreams of Pharaoh are one; God has shown Pharaoh what He is about to do:"
1_41_26: The seven good cows are seven years, and the seven good heads are seven years; the dreams are one.
1_41_27: And the seven thin and ugly cows which came up after them are seven years, and the seven thin heads blighted by the east wind are seven years of famine.
1_41_28: This is the thing which I have spoken to Pharaoh. God has shown Pharaoh what He is about to do.
1_41_29: Indeed seven years of great plenty will come throughout all the land of Egypt;
1_41_30: but after them seven years of famine will arise, and all the plenty will be forgotten in the land of Egypt; and the famine will ravage and destroy the land.
1_41_31: So the plenty will not be known in the land because of the famine following, for it will be very severe.
1_41_32: And the dream was repeated to Pharaoh twice because the thing is established by God, and God will shortly bring it to pass.
1_41_33: "Now therefore, let Pharaoh select a discerning and wise man, and set him over the land of Egypt."
1_41_34: Let Pharaoh do this, and let him appoint overseers over the land, to collect one-fifth of the produce of the land of Egypt in the seven plentiful years.
1_41_35: And let them gather all the food of those good years that are coming, and store up grain under the authority of Pharaoh, and let them keep food in the cities.
1_41_36: Then that food shall be as a reserve for the land for the seven years of famine which shall be in the land of Egypt, that the land may not perish during the famine."
1_41_37: So the advice was good in the eyes of Pharaoh and in the eyes of all his servants.
1_41_38: And Pharaoh said to his servants, "Can we find such a one as this, a man in whom is the Spirit of God?"
1_41_39: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Inasmuch as God has shown you all this, there is no one as discerning and wise as you."
1_41_40: You shall be over my house, and all my people shall be ruled according to your word; only in regard to the throne will I be greater than you."
1_41_41: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, "See, I have set you over all the land of Egypt."
1_41_42: Then Pharaoh took his signet ring off his hand and put it on Joseph's hand; and he clothed him in garments of fine linen and put a gold chain around his neck.
1_41_43: And he had him ride in his second chariot which he had; and they cried out before him, "Make way!" So he set him over all the land of Egypt.
1_41_44: Pharaoh also said to Joseph, "I am Pharaoh, and without your consent no man may lift his hand or foot in all the land of Egypt."
1_41_45: And Pharaoh called Joseph's name Zaphenath-Paneah. And he gave him as a wife Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On. So Joseph went out over all the land of Egypt.
1_41_46: Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh king of Egypt and entered his service. And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh and traveled throughout all the land of Egypt, inspecting it.
1_41_47: During the seven plentiful years the earth brought forth abundantly, producing outstanding harvests as the land yielded in great abundance.
1_41_48: So Joseph gathered all the food of these seven years which occurred in the land of Egypt and stored the grain in the cities, placing the surplus from the surrounding fields in each city.
1_41_49: Joseph stored up grain in great abundance like the sand of the sea, until he stopped measuring it, for it was beyond measure and could not be counted.
1_41_50: Before the years of famine came, two sons were born to Joseph, whom Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On, bore to him.
1_41_51: Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh: 'For God has made me forget all my trouble and all my father's household.'
1_41_52: And the name of the second he called Ephraim: 'For God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.'
1_41_53: Then the seven years of plenty which were in the land of Egypt came to an end,
1_41_54: and the seven years of famine began to come, just as Joseph had said. There was famine in all the surrounding lands, but throughout all the land of Egypt there was bread.
1_41_55: So when all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread. Then Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, 'Go to Joseph; whatever he says to you, do.'
1_41_56: And the famine spread over all the face of the earth, so Joseph opened all the storehouses and sold to the Egyptians, for the famine became severe in the land of Egypt.
1_41_57: So all countries came to Egypt to Joseph to buy grain, because the famine was severe in all lands.
1_42_1: When Jacob learned that grain was available in Egypt, he said to his sons, "Why do you keep looking at one another? Why are you standing around looking at one another?"
1_42_2: He said, "I have heard that there is grain in Egypt. Go down there and buy grain for us from that place, so that we may live and not die."
1_42_3: So Joseph's ten brothers went down to buy grain from Egypt.
1_42_4: But Jacob did not send Joseph's brother Benjamin with his brothers, for he said, "I am afraid that harm may befall him."
1_42_5: So the sons of Israel came to buy grain among those who journeyed, for the famine was in the land of Canaan.
1_42_6: Now Joseph was governor over the land; he was the one who sold to all the people of the land. And Joseph's brothers came and bowed down before him with their faces to the ground.
1_42_7: Joseph saw his brothers and recognized them, but he treated them like strangers and spoke harshly to them. "Where do you come from?" he asked. They said, "From the land of Canaan to buy food."
1_42_8: Joseph recognized his brothers, but they did not recognize him.
1_42_9: Then Joseph remembered the dreams which he had dreamed about them, and said to them, "You are spies; you have come to see the undefended parts of our land."
1_42_10: And they said to him, "No, my lord, but your servants have come to buy food."
1_42_11: We are all sons of one man; we are honest men, your servants are not spies."
1_42_12: But he said to them, "No, it is the undefended parts of our land that you have come to see."
1_42_13: And they said, "Your servants are twelve brothers, the sons of one man in the land of Canaan; and behold, the youngest is with our father today, and one is no more."
1_42_14: But Joseph said to them, "It is as I said to you: You are spies!"
1_42_15: By this you shall be tested: by the life of Pharaoh, you shall not leave this place unless your youngest brother comes here."
1_42_16: Send one of you, and let him bring your brother; and you shall be kept in prison, that your words may be tested to see whether there is any truth in you; or else, by the life of Pharaoh, surely you are spies."
1_42_17: So he put them all together in prison for three days.
1_42_18: On the third day Joseph said to them, "Do this and you will live, for I fear God."
1_42_19: If you are honest men, let one of your brothers be confined in your prison; but you, go and carry grain for the famine of your houses."
1_42_20: And bring your youngest brother to me; so your words will be verified, and you shall not die." And they did so.
1_42_21: Then they said to one another, "Truly we are guilty concerning our brother, because we saw the distress of his soul when he pleaded with us, yet we would not listen; therefore this distress has come upon us."
1_42_22: And Reuben answered them, saying, "Did I not tell you, 'Do not sin against the boy'; and you would not listen? Now comes the reckoning for his blood."
1_42_23: They did not know that Joseph understood them, for there was an interpreter between them.
1_42_24: He turned away from them and wept. Then he returned to them and spoke to them, and he took Simeon from them and bound him before their eyes.
1_42_25: Then Joseph gave orders to fill their bags with grain, to restore every man's money to his sack, and to give them provisions for the journey. This was done for them.
1_42_26: So they loaded their donkeys with their grain and departed from there.
1_42_27: And as one of them opened his sack to give his donkey fodder at the lodging place, he saw his money in the mouth of his sack.
1_42_28: So he said to his brothers, "My money has been restored, and there it is, in my sack!" Then their hearts failed them and they turned trembling to one another, saying, "What is this that God has done to us?"
1_42_29: When they came to Jacob their father in the land of Canaan, they told him all that had happened to them, saying,
1_42_30: "The man who is lord of the land spoke harshly to us and took us for spies of the country."
1_42_31: But we said to him, 'We are honest men; we are not spies."
1_42_32: We are twelve brothers, sons of our father; one is no more, and the youngest is with our father today in the land of Canaan.'"
1_42_33: Then the man, the lord of the land, said to us, 'By this I will know that you are honest men: Leave one of your brothers here with me, take grain for the famine of your households, and go your way."
1_42_34: Bring your youngest brother to me. Then I will know that you are not spies but honest men, and I will give your brother to you, and you may trade in the land.'"
1_42_35: Then it came about as they were emptying their sacks that, behold, every man's bundle of money was in his sack; and when they and their father saw the bundles of money, they were afraid.
1_42_36: And Jacob their father said to them, "You have bereaved me of my children: Joseph is no more, Simeon is no more, and you want to take Benjamin. All these things are against me."
1_42_37: Then Reuben spoke to his father, saying, "You may put my two sons to death if I do not bring him back to you; put him in my hands, and I will bring him back to you."
1_42_38: But he said, "My son shall not go down with you; for his brother is dead, and he is left alone. If harm should befall him on the journey you are taking, then you will bring down my gray hair with sorrow to the grave."
1_43_1: Now the famine was severe in the land.
1_43_2: And when they had eaten up the grain which they had brought from Egypt, their father said to them, 'Go back, buy us a little food.'
1_43_3: But Judah spoke to him, saying, 'The man solemnly warned us, saying, "You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you."'
1_43_4: If you send our brother with us, we will go down and buy you food.
1_43_5: But if you will not send him, we will not go down; for the man said to us, 'You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you.'"
1_43_6: And Israel said, 'Why did you deal so wrongfully with me as to tell the man whether you had still another brother?'
1_43_7: But they said, 'The man questioned us pointedly about ourselves and our family, saying, "Is your father still alive? Have you another brother?" And we told him according to these words. Could we possibly have known that he would say, "Bring your brother down"?'
1_43_8: Then Judah said to Israel his father, 'Send the lad with me, and we will arise and go, that we may live and not die, both we and you and also our little ones.'
1_43_9: I myself will be surety for him; from my hand you shall require him. If I do not bring him back to you and set him before you, then let me bear the blame forever.'
1_43_10: For if we had not lingered, surely by now we would have returned this second time.'"
1_43_11: And their father Israel said to them, 'If it must be so, then do this: Take some of the best fruits of the land in your vessels and carry down a present for the man—a little balm and a little honey, spices and myrrh, pistachio nuts and almonds.'
1_43_12: Take double money in your hand, and take back in your hand the money that was returned in the mouth of your sacks; perhaps it was an oversight.'
1_43_13: Take your brother also, and arise, go back to the man.'
1_43_14: And may God Almighty give you mercy before the man, that he may release your other brother and Benjamin. If I am bereaved, I am bereaved!'"
1_43_15: So the men took that present and Benjamin, and they took double money in their hand, and arose and went down to Egypt; and they stood before Joseph.
1_43_16: When Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the steward of his house, 'Take these men to my home, and slaughter an animal and make ready; for these men will dine with me at noon.'
1_43_17: Then the man did as Joseph ordered, and the man brought the men into Joseph's house.
1_43_18: Now the men were afraid because they were brought into Joseph's house; and they said, 'It is because of the money, which was returned in our sacks the first time, that we are brought in, so that he may make a case against us and seize us, to take us as slaves with our donkeys.'
1_43_19: When they drew near to the steward of Joseph's house, they talked with him at the door of the house,
1_43_20: and said, 'O sir, we indeed came down the first time to buy food;'
1_43_21: but it happened, when we came to the encampment, that we opened our sacks, and there, each man's money was in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight; so we have brought it back in our hand.'
1_43_22: And we have brought down other money in our hands to buy food. We do not know who put our money in our sacks.'"
1_43_23: But he said, 'Peace be with you, do not be afraid. Your God and the God of your father has given you treasure in your sacks; I had your money.' Then he brought Simeon out to them.
1_43_24: So the man brought the men into Joseph's house and gave them water, and they washed their feet; and he gave their donkeys feed.
1_43_25: Then they made the present ready for Joseph's coming at noon, for they heard that they would eat bread there.
1_43_26: And when Joseph came home, they brought him the present which was in their hand into the house, and bowed down before him to the earth.
1_43_27: Then he asked them about their well-being, and said, 'Is your father well, the old man of whom you spoke? Is he still alive?'
1_43_28: And they answered, 'Your servant our father is in good health; he is still alive.' And they bowed their heads down and prostrated themselves.
1_43_29: Then he lifted his eyes and saw his brother Benjamin, his mother's son, and said, 'Is this your younger brother of whom you spoke to me?' And he said, 'God be gracious to you, my son.'
1_43_30: Now his heart yearned for his brother; so Joseph made haste and sought somewhere to weep. And he went into his chamber and wept there.
1_43_31: Then he washed his face and came out; and he restrained himself, and said, 'Serve the bread.'
1_43_32: So they set him a place by himself, and them by themselves, and the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves; because the Egyptians could not eat food with the Hebrews, for that is an abomination to the Egyptians.
1_43_33: And they sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright and the youngest according to his youth; and the men looked in astonishment at one another.
1_43_34: Then he took servings to them from before him, but Benjamin's serving was five times as much as any of theirs. So they drank and were merry with him.
1_44_1: And he commanded the steward of his house, saying, "Fill the men's sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put each man's money in the mouth of his sack."
1_44_2: Also put my cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the sack of the youngest, and his grain money." So he did according to the word that Joseph had spoken.
1_44_3: As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away with their donkeys.
1_44_4: When they had gone out of the city and were not yet far off, Joseph said to his steward, "Get up, follow the men; and when you overtake them, say to them, 'Why have you repaid evil for good?'"
1_44_5: Is not this the cup from which my lord drinks, and by which he practices divination? You have done evil in doing this.'"
1_44_6: So he overtook them, and he spoke to them these same words.
1_44_7: And they said to him, "Why does my lord say these words? Far be it from your servants that they should do such a thing!"
1_44_8: Behold, we brought back to you from the land of Canaan the money which we found in the mouth of our sacks. How then could we steal silver or gold from your lord's house?"
1_44_9: With whomever of your servants it is found, let him die, and we also will be my lord's slaves."
1_44_10: And he said, "Now also let it be according to your words; he with whom it is found shall be my slave, and you shall be blameless."
1_44_11: Then each man quickly lowered his sack to the ground, and each opened his sack.
1_44_12: So he searched, beginning with the oldest and ending with the youngest, and the cup was found in Benjamin's sack.
1_44_13: Then they tore their clothes, and each man loaded his donkey and returned to the city.
1_44_14: When Judah and his brothers came to Joseph's house, he was still there, and they fell to the ground before him.
1_44_15: And Joseph said to them, "What deed is this you have done? Did you not know that such a man as I can certainly practice divination?"
1_44_16: Then Judah said, "What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak? Or how shall we clear ourselves? God has found out the iniquity of your servants; behold, we are my lord's slaves, both we and he also in whose hand the cup has been found."
1_44_17: But he said, "Far be it from me that I should do so; the man in whose hand the cup was found, he shall be my slave. And as for you, go up in peace to your father."
1_44_18: Then Judah came near to him and said: "O my lord, please let your servant speak a word in my lord's hearing, and do not let your anger burn against your servant; for you are even like Pharaoh."
1_44_19: My lord asked his servants, saying, 'Have you a father or a brother?'"
1_44_20: And we said to my lord, 'We have a father, an old man, and a young child born to him in his old age. His brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother's children, and his father loves him.'"
1_44_21: Then you said to your servants, 'Bring him down to me, that I may set my eyes on him.'"
1_44_22: And we said to my lord, 'The lad cannot leave his father, for if he should leave his father, his father would die.'"
1_44_23: But you said to your servants, 'Unless your youngest brother comes down with you, you shall see my face no more.'"
1_44_24: So it was, when we went up to your servant my father, that we told him the words of my lord."
1_44_25: And our father said, 'Go back and buy us a little food.'"
1_44_26: But we said, 'We cannot go down; if our youngest brother is with us, then we will go down; for we may not see the man's face unless our youngest brother is with us.'"
1_44_27: Then your servant my father said to us, 'You know that my wife bore me two sons;"
1_44_28: and the one went out from me, and I said, "Surely he is torn to pieces"; and I have not seen him since."
1_44_29: But if you take this one also from me, and calamity befalls him, you shall bring down my gray hair with sorrow to Sheol.'"
1_44_30: Now therefore, when I come to your servant my father, and the lad is not with us, since his life is bound up in the lad's life,"
1_44_31: it will happen, when he sees that the lad is not with us, that he will die. So your servants will bring down the gray hair of your servant our father with sorrow to Sheol."
1_44_32: For your servant became surety for the lad to my father, saying, 'If I do not bring him back to you, then I shall bear the blame before my father forever.'"
1_44_33: Now therefore, please let your servant remain instead of the lad as a slave to my lord, and let the lad go up with his brothers."
1_44_34: For how shall I go up to my father if the lad is not with me, lest perhaps I see the evil that would come upon my father?"
1_45_1: Then Joseph could not restrain himself before all those who stood by him, and he cried out, 'Make everyone go out from me!' So no one stood with him while Joseph made himself known to his brothers.
1_45_2: He wept aloud, so that the Egyptians heard it, and the household of Pharaoh heard it.
1_45_3: Then Joseph said to his brothers, 'I am Joseph! Is my father still alive?' But his brothers could not answer him, for they were dismayed at his presence.
1_45_4: And Joseph said to his brothers, 'Please come near to me.' So they came near. Then he said: 'I am Joseph your brother, whom you sold into Egypt.'
1_45_5: But now, do not be grieved or angry with yourselves because you sold me here, for God sent me before you to preserve life.
1_45_6: For these two years the famine has been in the land, and there are still five years in which there will be neither plowing nor harvesting.
1_45_7: And God sent me before you to preserve for you a remnant on the earth, and to save your lives by a great deliverance.
1_45_8: So now it was not you who sent me here, but God; and He has made me a father to Pharaoh, and lord of all his household, and a ruler over all the land of Egypt.
1_45_9: 'Hurry and go up to my father, and say to him, Thus says your son Joseph: God has made me lord of all Egypt; come down to me, do not delay.'
1_45_10: You shall dwell in the land of Goshen, and you shall be near to me—you and your children and your grandchildren, your flocks and your herds, and all that you have.
1_45_11: There I will provide for you, lest you and your household and all that you have come to poverty, for there are still five years of famine.'
1_45_12: And behold, your eyes and the eyes of my brother Benjamin see that it is my mouth that speaks to you.
1_45_13: So you shall tell my father of all my glory in Egypt, and of all that you have seen; and you shall hurry and bring my father down here.'
1_45_14: Then he fell on his brother Benjamin's neck and wept, and Benjamin wept on his neck.
1_45_15: Moreover he kissed all his brothers and wept over them, and after that his brothers talked with him.
1_45_16: Now the report of it was heard in Pharaoh's house, saying, 'Joseph's brothers have come.' So it pleased Pharaoh and his servants well.
1_45_17: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, 'Say to your brothers, Do this: Load your animals and depart; go to the land of Canaan.'
1_45_18: Bring your father and your households and come to me; I will give you the best of the land of Egypt, and you will eat the fat of the land.'
1_45_19: Now you are commanded—do this: Take carts out of the land of Egypt for your little ones and your wives; bring your father and come.
1_45_20: Also do not be concerned about your goods, for the best of all the land of Egypt is yours.'"
1_45_21: Then the sons of Israel did so; and Joseph gave them carts according to the command of Pharaoh, and he gave them provisions for the journey.
1_45_22: He gave to each of them changes of garments; but to Benjamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver and five changes of garments.
1_45_23: And he sent to his father these things: ten donkeys loaded with the good things of Egypt, and ten female donkeys loaded with grain, bread, and food for his father on the journey.
1_45_24: So he sent his brothers away, and they departed; and he said to them, 'See that you do not quarrel along the way.'
1_45_25: Then they went up out of Egypt, and came to the land of Canaan to Jacob their father.
1_45_26: And they told him, saying, 'Joseph is still alive, and he is governor over all the land of Egypt.' And Jacob's heart stood still, because he did not believe them.
1_45_27: But when they told him all the words which Joseph had said to them, and when he saw the carts which Joseph had sent to carry him, the spirit of Jacob their father revived.
1_45_28: Then Israel said, 'It is enough. Joseph my son is still alive. I will go and see him before I die.'
1_46_1: So Israel set out with all that he had and came to Beersheba, where he offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac.
1_46_2: That night God spoke to Israel in visions and said, 'Jacob, Jacob!' And he said, 'Here I am.'
1_46_3: God said, 'I am God, the God of your father; do not be afraid to go down to Egypt, for I will make you a great nation there.'
1_46_4: I will go down with you to Egypt, and I will also surely bring you back again; and Joseph will close your eyes.'
1_46_5: Then Jacob arose from Beersheba; and the sons of Israel carried their father Jacob, their little ones, and their wives, in the wagons that Pharaoh had sent to carry him.
1_46_6: They took their livestock and their goods, which they had acquired in the land of Canaan, and came to Egypt, Jacob and all his descendants with him.
1_46_7: His sons and his grandsons, his daughters and his granddaughters, and all his offspring he brought with him to Egypt.
1_46_8: Now these are the names of the descendants of Israel—Jacob and his sons—who came to Egypt: Reuben was Jacob's firstborn.
1_46_9: The sons of Reuben were Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
1_46_10: The sons of Simeon were Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, the son of a Canaanite woman.
1_46_11: The sons of Levi were Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
1_46_12: The sons of Judah were Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah (but Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan). The sons of Perez were Hezron and Hamul.
1_46_13: The sons of Issachar were Tola, Puvah, Job, and Shimron.
1_46_14: The sons of Zebulun were Sered, Elon, and Jahleel.
1_46_15: These were the sons of Leah, whom she bore to Jacob in Paddan-aram, with his daughter Dinah. All the persons, his sons and daughters, were thirty-three.
1_46_16: The sons of Gad were Ziphion, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi, and Areli.
1_46_17: The sons of Asher were Jimnah, Ishuah, Isui, Beriah, and Serah their sister. The sons of Beriah were Heber and Malchiel.
1_46_18: These were the sons of Zilpah, whom Laban gave to Leah his daughter; and she bore these to Jacob: sixteen persons.
1_46_19: The sons of Rachel, Jacob's wife, were Joseph and Benjamin.
1_46_20: To Joseph in the land of Egypt were born Manasseh and Ephraim, whom Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, priest of On, bore to him.
1_46_21: The sons of Benjamin were Bela, Becher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard.
1_46_22: These were the sons of Rachel, who were born to Jacob: fourteen persons in all.
1_46_23: The son of Dan was Hushim.
1_46_24: The sons of Naphtali were Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem.
1_46_25: These were the sons of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to Rachel his daughter, and she bore these to Jacob: seven persons in all.
1_46_26: All the persons who came with Jacob to Egypt, who were his direct descendants, not including the wives of his sons, were sixty-six persons in all.
1_46_27: And the sons of Joseph, who were born to him in Egypt, were two persons. All the persons of the house of Jacob who came to Egypt were seventy.
1_46_28: Now Jacob sent Judah ahead of him to Joseph to show the way to Goshen, and they came into the land of Goshen.
1_46_29: Joseph prepared his chariot and went up to Goshen to meet his father Israel; and when he presented himself to him, he fell on his neck and wept a long time.
1_46_30: Israel said to Joseph, 'Now let me die, since I have seen your face, because you are still alive.'
1_46_31: Then Joseph said to his brothers and to his father's household, 'I will go up and tell Pharaoh, and say to him, My brothers and those of my father's house, who were in the land of Canaan, have come to me.'
1_46_32: And the men are shepherds, for their occupation has been to feed livestock; and they have brought their flocks, their herds, and all that they have.'
1_46_33: So it shall be, when Pharaoh calls you and says, 'What is your occupation?'
1_46_34: that you shall say, 'Your servants have been keepers of livestock from our youth even until now, both we and our fathers,' that you may dwell in the land of Goshen; for every shepherd is an abomination to the Egyptians.'
1_47_1: Then Joseph went and told Pharaoh, saying, "My father and my brothers, with their flocks and herds and all that they possess, have come from the land of Canaan; and behold, they are now in the land of Goshen."
1_47_2: And from among his brothers he took five men and presented them to Pharaoh.
1_47_3: Then Pharaoh said to his brothers, "What is your occupation?" And they said to Pharaoh, "Your servants are shepherds, both we and our fathers."
1_47_4: They said to Pharaoh, "We have come to sojourn in the land, for there is no pasture for your servants' flocks, for the famine is severe in the land of Canaan. Now therefore, please let your servants dwell in the land of Goshen."
1_47_5: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Your father and your brothers have come to you."
1_47_6: The land of Egypt is before you. Settle your father and your brothers in the best of the land; let them dwell in the land of Goshen. And if you know any capable men among them, put them in charge of my livestock."
1_47_7: Then Joseph brought in Jacob his father and presented him to Pharaoh, and Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
1_47_8: Pharaoh said to Jacob, "How old are you?"
1_47_9: And Jacob said to Pharaoh, "The years of my pilgrimage are one hundred and thirty; few and hard have been the days of the years of my life, and they have not attained to the days of the years of my fathers in the days of their pilgrimage."
1_47_10: And Jacob blessed Pharaoh, and went out from his presence.
1_47_11: Then Joseph settled his father and his brothers and gave them a possession in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of Rameses, as Pharaoh had commanded.
1_47_12: And Joseph provided his father, his brothers, and all his father's household with food, according to the number of their dependents.
1_47_13: Now there was no food in all the land; for the famine was very severe, so that the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan languished because of the famine.
1_47_14: And Joseph gathered all the money that was found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan, for the grain which they bought; and Joseph brought the money into Pharaoh's house.
1_47_15: When the money was all spent in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan, all the Egyptians came to Joseph and said, "Give us food, for why should we die before your eyes? For our money is gone."
1_47_16: Then Joseph said, "Give your livestock, and I will give you food for your livestock, if the money is gone."
1_47_17: So they brought their livestock to Joseph, and Joseph gave them food in exchange for the horses, the flocks, the herds, and the donkeys. Thus he fed them with food in exchange for all their livestock that year.
1_47_18: When that year was ended, they came to him the next year and said, "We will not hide from my lord that our money is gone; my lord also has our herds of livestock. There is nothing left in the sight of my lord but our bodies and our lands."
1_47_19: Why should we die before your eyes, both we and our land? Buy us and our land for food, and we and our land will be servants to Pharaoh; give us seed, that we may live and not die, that the land may not be desolate."
1_47_20: So Joseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh; for every man of the Egyptians sold his field, because the famine was severe upon them. Thus the land became Pharaoh's.
1_47_21: And as for the people, he relocated them to cities from one end of Egypt's border to the other.
1_47_22: Only the land of the priests he did not buy; for the priests had an allotment from Pharaoh, and they ate their portion which Pharaoh gave them; therefore they did not sell their lands.
1_47_23: Then Joseph said to the people, "Behold, I have bought you and your land this day for Pharaoh. Now here is seed for you, and you shall sow the land."
1_47_24: And it shall come to pass in the harvest that you shall give one-fifth to Pharaoh. Four-fifths shall be your own, as seed for the field and for your food, for those of your households and as food for your little ones."
1_47_25: So they said, "You have saved our lives; let us find favor in the sight of my lord, and we will be Pharaoh's servants."
1_47_26: And Joseph made it a law over the land of Egypt to this day, that Pharaoh should have one-fifth, except for the land of the priests only, which did not become Pharaoh's.
1_47_27: So Israel dwelt in the land of Egypt, in the country of Goshen; and they had possessions there and became fruitful and multiplied exceedingly.
1_47_28: And Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years. So the length of Jacob's life was one hundred and forty-seven years.
1_47_29: When the time drew near that Israel must die, he called his son Joseph and said to him, "Now if I have found favor in your sight, please put your hand under my thigh, and deal kindly and truly with me. Please do not bury me in Egypt,"
1_47_30: but let me lie with my fathers; you shall carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their burial place." And he said, "I will do as you have said."
1_47_31: Then he said, "Swear to me." And he swore to him. So Israel bowed himself in worship at the head of his bed.
1_48_1: Now it came to pass after these things that Joseph was told, 'Indeed your father is sick'; so he took with him his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim.
1_48_2: When Jacob was told, 'Look, your son Joseph has come to you,' Israel strengthened himself and sat up on the bed.
1_48_3: Then Jacob said to Joseph: 'God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan and blessed me,'
1_48_4: and said to me, 'Behold, I will make you fruitful and multiply you, and I will make of you a multitude of people and many nations. I will give this land to your descendants after you as an everlasting possession.'
1_48_5: Now your two sons, Ephraim and Manasseh, who were born to you in the land of Egypt before I came to you in Egypt, are mine; as Reuben and Simeon, they shall be mine.
1_48_6: Your offspring whom you beget after them shall be yours; they will be called by the name of their brothers in their inheritance.
1_48_7: As for me, when I came from Paddan, Rachel died beside me in the land of Canaan on the way, when there was still some distance to go to Ephrath; and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem).'
1_48_8: Then Israel saw Joseph's sons, and said, 'Who are these?'
1_48_9: Joseph said to his father, 'They are my sons, whom God has given me in this place.' And he said, 'Bring them to me, and I will bless them.'
1_48_10: Now the eyes of Israel were dim with age, so that he could not see. Joseph brought them near him, and he kissed them and embraced them.
1_48_11: And Israel said to Joseph, 'I never expected to see your face; but now God has also shown me your offspring!'
1_48_12: Joseph brought them from beside his knees, and he bowed down with his face to the earth.
1_48_13: Joseph took them both, Ephraim with his right hand toward Israel's left hand, and Manasseh with his left hand toward Israel's right hand, and brought them near him.
1_48_14: Then Israel stretched out his right hand and laid it on Ephraim's head, who was the younger, and his left hand on Manasseh's head, crossing his hands knowingly, for Manasseh was the firstborn.
1_48_15: And he blessed Joseph, and said: 'The God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, The God who has fed me all my life long to this day,'
1_48_16: The Angel who has redeemed me from all evil, bless the lads; Let my name be named upon them, And the name of my fathers Abraham and Isaac; And let them grow into a multitude in the midst of the earth.'
1_48_17: Now when Joseph saw that his father laid his right hand on the head of Ephraim, it displeased him; so he took hold of his father's hand to remove it from Ephraim's head to Manasseh's head.
1_48_18: And Joseph said to his father, 'Not so, my father, for this one is the firstborn; put your right hand on his head.'
1_48_19: But his father refused and said, 'I know, my son, I know. He also shall become a people, and he also shall be great; but truly his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his descendants shall become a multitude of nations.'
1_48_20: So he blessed them that day, saying, 'By you Israel will bless, saying, May God make you as Ephraim and as Manasseh!' And thus he set Ephraim before Manasseh.
1_48_21: Then Israel said to Joseph, 'Behold, I am dying, but God will be with you and bring you back to the land of your fathers.'
1_48_22: Moreover I have given to you one portion above your brothers, which I took from the hand of the Amorite with my sword and my bow.'
1_49_1: Then Jacob called his sons and said, 'Gather together, that I may tell you what shall befall you in the days to come.'
1_49_2: Gather together and hear, you sons of Jacob; listen to Israel your father.
1_49_3: Reuben, you are my firstborn, my might and the beginning of my strength, the firstfruits of my vigor, excelling in dignity and excelling in power.
1_49_4: Unstable as water, you shall not excel, because you went up to your father's bed; then you defiled it—he went up to my couch.
1_49_5: Simeon and Levi are brothers; their swords are weapons of violence and implements of cruelty.
1_49_6: Let not my soul enter their council; let not my honor be united to their assembly; for in their anger they slew men, and in their self-will they hamstrung oxen.
1_49_7: Cursed be their anger, for it is fierce; and their wrath, for it is cruel! I will divide them in Jacob and scatter them in Israel.
1_49_8: Judah, you are he whom your brothers shall praise; your hand shall be on the neck of your enemies; your father's sons shall bow down before you.
1_49_9: Judah is a lion's whelp; from the prey, my son, you have gone up. He couches down and lies down as a lion and as a lioness—who dares to rouse him?
1_49_10: The scepter shall not depart from Judah, nor the ruler's staff from between his feet, until Shiloh comes; and to him shall be the obedience of the peoples.
1_49_11: Binding his donkey to the vine and his donkey's colt to the choice vine, he washes his garments in wine and his robes in the blood of grapes.
1_49_12: His eyes are darker than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.
1_49_13: Zebulun shall dwell by the haven of the sea; he shall become a haven for ships, and his border shall adjoin Sidon.
1_49_14: Issachar is a strong donkey lying down between two burdens and the sheepfolds.
1_49_15: When he saw that a resting place was good and that the land was pleasant, he bowed his shoulder to bear a burden and became a servant at forced labor.
1_49_16: Dan shall judge his people as one of the tribes of Israel.
1_49_17: Dan shall be a serpent by the way, a viper by the path, that bites the horse's heels so that its rider falls backward.
1_49_18: I have waited for your salvation, O LORD.
1_49_19: Gad, a troop shall tramp upon him, but he shall triumph at their heels.
1_49_20: Bread from Asher shall be rich, and he shall yield royal dainties.
1_49_21: Naphtali is a doe let loose that bears beautiful fawns.
1_49_22: Joseph is a fruitful bough, a fruitful bough by a well; his branches run over the wall.
1_49_23: The archers have bitterly grieved him, shot at him and hated him.
1_49_24: But his bow remained in strength, and the arms of his hands were made strong by the hands of the Mighty God of Jacob (from there is the Shepherd, the Stone of Israel).
1_49_25: By the God of your father who will help you, and by the Almighty who will bless you with blessings of heaven above, blessings of the deep that lies beneath, blessings of the breasts and of the womb.
1_49_26: The blessings of your father have excelled the blessings of my ancestors, up to the utmost bound of the everlasting hills. They shall be on the head of Joseph, and on the crown of the head of him who was separate from his brothers.
1_49_27: Benjamin is a ravenous wolf; in the morning he shall devour the prey, and at night he shall divide the spoil.
1_49_28: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what their father spoke to them. And he blessed them; he blessed each one according to his own blessing.
1_49_29: Then he charged them and said to them: 'I am to be gathered to my people; bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite.'
1_49_30: In the cave that is in the field of Machpelah, which is before Mamre in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field of Ephron the Hittite as a possession for a burial place.
1_49_31: There they buried Abraham and Sarah his wife, there they buried Isaac and Rebekah his wife, and there I buried Leah.
1_49_32: The field and the cave that is there were purchased from the sons of Heth.
1_49_33: And when Jacob had finished commanding his sons, he drew his feet up into the bed and breathed his last, and was gathered to his people.
1_50_1: Then Joseph fell on his father's face and wept over him and kissed him tenderly.
1_50_2: Joseph commanded his servants who were physicians to embalm his father. So the physicians embalmed Israel.
1_50_3: Forty days were required for it, for that is the time required for embalming. And the Egyptians wept for him seventy days.
1_50_4: When the days of mourning were past, Joseph spoke to the household of Pharaoh, saying, 'If now I have found favor in your eyes, please speak in the hearing of Pharaoh, saying,'
1_50_5: 'My father made me swear, saying, "Behold, I am dying; in my grave which I dug for myself in the land of Canaan, there you shall bury me." Now therefore, please let me go up and bury my father, and I will return.'"
1_50_6: Pharaoh said, 'Go up and bury your father, as he made you swear.'
1_50_7: So Joseph went up to bury his father; and with him went up all the servants of Pharaoh, the elders of his household, and all the elders of the land of Egypt,
1_50_8: as well as all the household of Joseph, his brothers, and his father's house. Only their little ones, their flocks, and their herds they left in the land of Goshen.
1_50_9: And there went up with him both chariots and horsemen. It was a very great company.
1_50_10: When they came to the threshing floor of Atad, which is beyond the Jordan, they lamented there with a very great and solemn lamentation. Joseph observed seven days of mourning for his father.
1_50_11: When the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, saw the mourning at the threshing floor of Atad, they said, 'This is a grievous mourning of the Egyptians.' Therefore its name was called Abel Mizraim, which is beyond the Jordan.
1_50_12: So his sons did for him just as he had commanded them.
1_50_13: For his sons carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the cave of the field of Machpelah, before Mamre, which Abraham bought with the field from Ephron the Hittite as property for a burial place.
1_50_14: After he had buried his father, Joseph returned to Egypt, he and his brothers and all who went up with him to bury his father.
1_50_15: When Joseph's brothers saw that their father was dead, they said, 'Perhaps Joseph will hate us and may actually repay us for all the evil which we did to him.'
1_50_16: So they sent messengers to Joseph, saying, 'Your father commanded before he died, saying,'
1_50_17: 'Thus you shall say to Joseph: "I beg you, please forgive the transgression of your brothers and their sin; for they did evil to you."' Now, please forgive the transgression of the servants of the God of your father.' And Joseph wept when they spoke to him.
1_50_18: Then his brothers also went and fell down before his face, and they said, 'Behold, we are your servants.'
1_50_19: Joseph said to them, 'Do not be afraid, for am I in the place of God?'
1_50_20: As for you, you meant evil against me; but God meant it for good, in order to bring it about as it is this day, to save many people alive.
1_50_21: Now therefore, do not be afraid; I will provide for you and your little ones.' And he comforted them and spoke kindly to them.
1_50_22: So Joseph dwelt in Egypt, he and his father's household. And Joseph lived one hundred and ten years.
1_50_23: Joseph saw Ephraim's children to the third generation. The children of Machir, the son of Manasseh, were also brought up on Joseph's knees.
1_50_24: And Joseph said to his brothers, 'I am dying; but God will surely visit you, and bring you out of this land to the land of which He swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.'
1_50_25: Then Joseph took an oath from the children of Israel, saying, 'God will surely visit you, and you shall carry up my bones from here.'
1_50_26: So Joseph died, being one hundred and ten years old; and they embalmed him, and he was put in a coffin in Egypt.
2_1_1: Now these are the names of the sons of Israel who came to Egypt with Jacob, each with his household:
2_1_4: Dan and Naphtali, Gad, and Asher.
2_1_5: All the descendants of Jacob were seventy persons in number; Joseph was already in Egypt.
2_1_6: Then Joseph died, and all his brothers and all that generation.
2_1_7: But the children of Israel were fruitful and increased abundantly, multiplied and became exceedingly mighty, so that the land was filled with them.
2_1_8: Now there arose a new king over Egypt, who did not know Joseph.
2_1_9: And he said to his people, "Behold, the people of the children of Israel are more and mightier than we;
2_1_10: Come, let us deal shrewdly with them, lest they multiply and in the event of war they will join our enemies and fight against us and escape from the land."
2_1_11: Therefore they set taskmasters over them to afflict them with their burdens. They built for Pharaoh supply cities, Pithom and Raamses.
2_1_12: But the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and grew, and the more they spread abroad. And the Egyptians were in dread of the children of Israel.
2_1_13: So the Egyptians made the children of Israel serve with rigor.
2_1_14: And they made their lives bitter with hard bondage—in mortar and brick and in all manner of service in the field. All their service in which they made them serve was with rigor.
2_1_15: Then the king of Egypt spoke to the Hebrew midwives, one of whom was named Shiphrah and the other Puah;
2_1_16: and he said, "When you do the duties of a midwife for the Hebrew women and see them on the birthstools, if it is a son, then you shall kill him; but if it is a daughter, then she shall live."
2_1_17: But the midwives feared God and did not do as the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the male children alive.
2_1_18: So the king of Egypt called for the midwives and said to them, "Why have you done this thing and saved the male children alive?"
2_1_19: And the midwives said to Pharaoh, "Because the Hebrew women are not like the Egyptian women; for they are vigorous and give birth before the midwives come to them."
2_1_20: Therefore God dealt well with the midwives, and the people multiplied and grew very mighty.
2_1_21: And because the midwives feared God, He provided households for them.
2_1_22: So Pharaoh commanded all his people, saying, "Every son who is born you shall cast into the river, and every daughter you shall save alive."
2_2_1: Now a man from the house of Levi went and took as his wife a daughter of Levi.
2_2_2: The woman conceived and bore a son; and when she saw that he was beautiful, she hid him three months.
2_2_3: But when she could no longer hide him, she got a papyrus basket for him and coated it with tar and pitch. She put the child in it and laid it among the reeds by the bank of the Nile.
2_2_4: And his sister stood at a distance to know what would happen to him.
2_2_5: Then Pharaoh's daughter came down to bathe at the river, and her maidens walked along the riverbank. She saw the basket among the reeds and sent her maid to get it.
2_2_6: When she opened it, she saw the child, and behold, the baby was crying. She had compassion on him and said, "This is one of the Hebrews' children."
2_2_7: Then his sister said to Pharaoh's daughter, "Shall I go and call a nurse for you from the Hebrew women to nurse the child for you?"
2_2_8: And Pharaoh's daughter said to her, "Go." So the maiden went and called the child's mother.
2_2_9: Then Pharaoh's daughter said to her, "Take this child away and nurse him for me, and I will give you your wages." So the woman took the child and nursed him.
2_2_10: When the child grew, she brought him to Pharaoh's daughter and he became her son. She named him Moses, saying, "Because I drew him out of the water."
2_2_11: Now when Moses had grown up, he went out to his brethren and looked at their burdens. And he saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his brethren.
2_2_12: So he looked this way and that, and when he saw no one, he killed the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.
2_2_13: When he went out the next day, behold, two Hebrews were fighting, and he said to the one in the wrong, "Why are you striking your companion?"
2_2_14: But he said, "Who made you a prince and a judge over us? Do you intend to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?" Then Moses was afraid and said, "Surely this thing is known."
2_2_15: When Pharaoh heard of this matter, he sought to kill Moses. But Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh and dwelt in the land of Midian, and sat down by a well.
2_2_16: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters, and they came and drew water and filled the troughs to water their father's flock.
2_2_17: Then the shepherds came and drove them away, but Moses stood up and helped them and watered their flock.
2_2_18: When they came to Reuel their father, he said, "How is it that you have come so soon today?"
2_2_19: And they said, "An Egyptian delivered us from the hand of the shepherds, and he also drew water for us and watered the flock."
2_2_20: So he said to his daughters, "And where is he? Why is it that you have left the man? Call him, that he may eat bread."
2_2_21: Then Moses was content to dwell with the man, and he gave Zipporah his daughter to Moses in marriage.
2_2_22: And she bore him a son. He called his name Gershom, for he said, "I have been a stranger in a foreign land."
2_2_23: Now it came to pass in the course of those many days that the king of Egypt died. And the children of Israel groaned because of their bondage and cried out; and their cry came up to God because of the bondage.
2_2_24: So God heard their groaning, and God remembered His covenant with Abraham, with Isaac, and with Jacob.
2_2_25: And God looked upon the children of Israel, and God took notice of them.
2_3_1: Now Moses was tending the flock of Jethro his father-in-law, the priest of Midian. He led the flock to the back of the wilderness, and came to Horeb, the mountain of God.
2_3_2: And the angel of the Lord appeared to him in a flame of fire from the midst of a bush. He looked, and behold, the bush was burning with fire, yet the bush was not consumed.
2_3_3: Then Moses said, 'I will turn aside now and see this great sight, why the bush is not burned up.'
2_3_4: When the Lord saw that he turned aside to look, God called to him from the midst of the bush, and said, 'Moses, Moses!' And he said, 'Here I am.'
2_3_5: Then He said, 'Do not come near this place. Take your sandals off your feet, for the place where you stand is holy ground.'
2_3_6: Moreover He said, 'I am the God of your father—the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.' And Moses hid his face, for he was afraid to look upon God.
2_3_7: And the Lord said: 'I have surely seen the affliction of My people who are in Egypt, and have heard their cry because of their taskmasters, for I know their sufferings.'
2_3_8: So I have come down to deliver them out of the hand of the Egyptians, and to bring them up from that land to a good and spacious land, to a land flowing with milk and honey, to the place of the Canaanites and the Hittites and the Amorites and the Perizzites and the Hivites and the Jebusites.
2_3_9: Now therefore, behold, the cry of the children of Israel has come to Me, and I have also seen the oppression with which the Egyptians oppress them.
2_3_10: Come now therefore, and I will send you to Pharaoh that you may bring My people, the children of Israel, out of Egypt.'
2_3_11: But Moses said to God, 'Who am I that I should go to Pharaoh, and that I should bring the children of Israel out of Egypt?'
2_3_12: So He said, 'I will certainly be with you. And this shall be a sign to you that I have sent you: When you have brought the people out of Egypt, you shall serve God on this mountain.'
2_3_13: Then Moses said to God, 'Indeed, when I come to the children of Israel and say to them, "The God of your fathers has sent me to you," and they say to me, "What is His name?" what shall I say to them?'
2_3_14: And God said to Moses, 'I AM WHO I AM.' And He said, 'Thus you shall say to the children of Israel, "I AM has sent me to you."'
2_3_15: Moreover God said to Moses, 'Thus you shall say to the children of Israel: "The Lord God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has sent me to you. This is My name forever, and this is My memorial to all generations."'
2_3_16: Go and gather the elders of Israel together, and say to them, 'The Lord God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, appeared to me, saying, "I have surely visited you and seen what is done to you in Egypt;"'
2_3_17: and I have said I will bring you up out of the affliction of Egypt to the land of the Canaanites and the Hittites and the Amorites and the Perizzites and the Hivites and the Jebusites, to a land flowing with milk and honey.'"
2_3_18: Then they will heed your voice; and you shall come, you and the elders of Israel, to the king of Egypt; and you shall say to him, 'The Lord God of the Hebrews has met with us; and now, please, let us go three days' journey into the wilderness, that we may sacrifice to the Lord our God.'
2_3_19: But I am sure that the king of Egypt will not let you go, no, not unless by a mighty hand.
2_3_20: So I will stretch out My hand and strike Egypt with all My wonders which I will do in its midst; and after that he will let you go.
2_3_21: And I will give this people favor in the sight of the Egyptians; and it shall be, when you go, that you shall not go empty-handed.
2_3_22: But every woman shall ask of her neighbor, and of her who dwells in her house, articles of silver, articles of gold, and clothing; and you shall put them on your sons and on your daughters. So you shall plunder the Egyptians."
2_4_1: Then Moses answered, "But suppose they will not believe me or listen to my voice; suppose they say, 'The LORD has not appeared to you.'"
2_4_2: So the LORD said to him, "What is that in your hand?" He said, "A staff."
2_4_3: And He said, "Cast it on the ground." So he cast it on the ground, and it became a serpent; and Moses ran from it.
2_4_4: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Reach out your hand and take it by the tail"—so he stretched out his hand and caught it, and it became a staff in his hand.
2_4_5: "That they may believe that the LORD, the God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has appeared to you."
2_4_6: Furthermore the LORD said to him, "Now put your hand in your cloak." And he put his hand in his cloak, and when he took it out, behold, his hand was leprous, like snow.
2_4_7: And He said, "Put your hand in your cloak again." So he put his hand in his cloak again, and drew it out, and behold, it was restored like his other flesh.
2_4_8: "Then it will be, if they do not believe you, nor heed the message of the first sign, that they may believe the message of the latter sign."
2_4_9: And it shall be, if they do not believe even these two signs, or listen to your voice, that you shall take water from the river and pour it on the dry land. The water which you take from the river will become blood on the dry land."
2_4_10: Then Moses said to the LORD, "O my Lord, I am not eloquent, neither before nor since You have spoken to Your servant; but I am slow of speech and slow of tongue."
2_4_11: So the LORD said to him, "Who has made man's mouth? Or who makes the mute, the deaf, the seeing, or the blind? Have not I, the LORD?"
2_4_12: Now therefore, go, and I will be with your mouth and teach you what you shall say."
2_4_13: But he said, "O my Lord, please send by the hand of whomever else You may send."
2_4_14: So the anger of the LORD was kindled against Moses, and He said: "Is not Aaron the Levite your brother? I know that he can speak well. And look, he is also coming out to meet you. When he sees you, he will be glad in his heart."
2_4_15: Now you shall speak to him and put the words in his mouth. And I will be with your mouth and with his mouth, and I will teach you what you shall do."
2_4_16: So he shall be your spokesman to the people. And he himself shall be as a mouth for you, and you shall be to him as God."
2_4_17: And you shall take this staff in your hand, with which you shall do the signs."
2_4_18: So Moses went and returned to Jethro his father-in-law, and said to him, "Please let me go and return to my brethren who are in Egypt, and see whether they are still alive." And Jethro said to Moses, "Go in peace."
2_4_19: Now the LORD said to Moses in Midian, "Go, return to Egypt; for all the men who sought your life are dead."
2_4_20: Then Moses took his wife and his sons and set them on a donkey, and he returned to the land of Egypt. And Moses took the staff of God in his hand.
2_4_21: And the LORD said to Moses, "When you go back to Egypt, see that you do all those wonders before Pharaoh which I have put in your hand. But I will harden his heart, so that he will not let the people go."
2_4_22: Then you shall say to Pharaoh, 'Thus says the LORD: "Israel is My son, My firstborn."'"
2_4_23: So I say to you, let My son go that he may serve Me. But if you refuse to let him go, indeed I will kill your son, your firstborn.'"
2_4_24: And it came to pass on the way, at the encampment, that the LORD met him and sought to kill him.
2_4_25: Then Zipporah took a flint knife and cut off the foreskin of her son and touched Moses' feet with it, and said, "Surely you are a husband of blood to me!"
2_4_26: So He let him go. Then she said, "You are a husband of blood"—because of the circumcision.
2_4_27: And the LORD said to Aaron, "Go into the wilderness to meet Moses." So he went and met him on the mountain of God, and kissed him.
2_4_28: So Moses told Aaron all the words of the LORD who had sent him, and all the signs which He had commanded him.
2_4_29: Then Moses and Aaron went and gathered together all the elders of the children of Israel.
2_4_30: And Aaron spoke all the words which the LORD had spoken to Moses. Then he did the signs in the sight of the people.
2_4_31: So the people believed; and when they heard that the LORD had visited the children of Israel and that He had seen their affliction, then they bowed their heads and worshiped.
2_5_1: Afterward Moses and Aaron went and said to Pharaoh, 'Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Let my people go, that they may hold a feast to me in the wilderness.'
2_5_2: But Pharaoh said, 'Who is the LORD, that I should obey his voice to let Israel go? I do not know the LORD, and moreover, I will not let Israel go.'
2_5_3: Then they said, 'The God of the Hebrews has met with us. Please let us go a three days' journey into the wilderness that we may sacrifice to the LORD our God, lest he fall upon us with pestilence or with the sword.'
2_5_4: Then the king of Egypt said to them, 'Moses and Aaron, why do you take the people away from their work? Get back to your labor!'
2_5_5: And Pharaoh said, 'Look, the people of the land are now many, and you make them rest from their burdens.'
2_5_6: That same day Pharaoh commanded the taskmasters of the people and their foremen, saying,
2_5_7: 'You shall no longer give the people straw to make brick as before. Let them go and gather straw for themselves.'
2_5_8: 'But the number of bricks which they were making before, you shall impose on them; you shall by no means reduce it. For they are idle, therefore they cry out, saying, Let us go and sacrifice to our God.'
2_5_9: 'Let heavier work be laid on the men, that they may labor at it and pay no regard to lying words.'
2_5_10: So the taskmasters and the foremen of the people went out and said to the people, 'Thus says Pharaoh, I will not give you straw.'
2_5_11: 'Go and get your straw yourselves wherever you can find it, but your work will not be reduced in the least.'"
2_5_12: So the people were scattered throughout all the land of Egypt to gather stubble for straw.
2_5_13: The taskmasters were urgent, saying, 'Fulfill your work, your daily quota, as when there was straw.'
2_5_14: And the foremen of the people of Israel, whom Pharaoh's taskmasters had set over them, were beaten and were asked, 'Why have you not fulfilled your task in making brick both yesterday and today, as before?'
2_5_15: Then the foremen of the people of Israel came and cried out to Pharaoh, saying, 'Why do you deal thus with your servants?'
2_5_16: 'There is no straw given to your servants, and they say to us, Make brick! And behold, your servants are beaten, but the fault is in your own people.'
2_5_17: But he said, 'You are idle, you are idle; that is why you say, Let us go and sacrifice to the LORD.'
2_5_18: 'Go now and work; for no straw shall be given you, yet you must still deliver the same number of bricks.'
2_5_19: The foremen of the people of Israel saw that they were in trouble when they were told, 'You shall not reduce any bricks from your daily quota.'
2_5_20: When they left Pharaoh, they met Moses and Aaron, who stood waiting to meet them.
2_5_21: And they said to them, 'The LORD look upon you and judge, because you have made us abhorrent in the sight of Pharaoh and his servants, and have put a sword in their hand to kill us.'
2_5_22: Then Moses returned to the LORD and said, 'Lord, why have you brought trouble on this people? Why did you ever send me?'
2_5_23: 'Ever since I came to Pharaoh to speak in your name, he has done evil to this people, and you have not delivered your people at all.'
2_6_1: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Now you shall see what I will do to Pharaoh. For with a strong hand he will let them go, and with a strong hand he will drive them out of his land."
2_6_2: And God spoke to Moses and said to him: "I am the LORD."
2_6_3: I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, as God Almighty, but by My name LORD I was not known to them."
2_6_4: I have also established My covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land of their pilgrimage, in which they were strangers."
2_6_5: And I have also heard the groaning of the children of Israel whom the Egyptians keep in bondage, and I have remembered My covenant."
2_6_6: Therefore say to the children of Israel: 'I am the LORD; I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, I will rescue you from their bondage, and I will redeem you with an outstretched arm and with great judgments."
2_6_7: I will take you as My people, and I will be your God. Then you shall know that I am the LORD your God who brings you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians."
2_6_8: And I will bring you into the land which I swore to give to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob; and I will give it to you as a heritage: I am the LORD.'"
2_6_9: So Moses spoke thus to the children of Israel; but they did not heed Moses, because of anguish of spirit and cruel bondage.
2_6_10: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
2_6_11: "Go in, tell Pharaoh king of Egypt to let the children of Israel go out of his land."
2_6_12: And Moses spoke before the LORD, saying, "The children of Israel have not heeded me. How then shall Pharaoh heed me, for I am of uncircumcised lips?"
2_6_13: Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, and gave them a command for the children of Israel and for Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt.
2_6_14: These are the heads of their fathers' houses: The sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel, were Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi. These are the families of Reuben.
2_6_15: And the sons of Simeon were Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul the son of a Canaanite woman. These are the families of Simeon.
2_6_16: These are the names of the sons of Levi according to their generations: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. And the years of the life of Levi were one hundred and thirty-seven.
2_6_17: The sons of Gershon were Libni and Shimei according to their families.
2_6_18: And the sons of Kohath were Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel. And the years of the life of Kohath were one hundred and thirty-three.
2_6_19: The sons of Merari were Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of Levi according to their generations.
2_6_20: Now Amram took for himself Jochebed, his father's sister, as wife; and she bore him Aaron and Moses. And the years of the life of Amram were one hundred and thirty-seven.
2_6_21: The sons of Izhar were Korah, Nepheg, and Zichri.
2_6_22: And the sons of Uzziel were Mishael, Elzaphan, and Sithri.
2_6_23: Aaron took to himself Elisheba, daughter of Amminadab, sister of Nahshon, as wife; and she bore him Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
2_6_24: And the sons of Korah were Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph. These are the families of the Korahites.
2_6_25: Eleazar, Aaron's son, took for himself one of the daughters of Putiel as wife; and she bore him Phinehas. These are the heads of the fathers' houses of the Levites according to their families.
2_6_26: These are the same Aaron and Moses to whom the LORD said, "Bring out the children of Israel from the land of Egypt according to their armies."
2_6_27: These are the ones who spoke to Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring out the children of Israel from Egypt. These are the same Moses and Aaron.
2_6_28: And it came to pass, on the day the LORD spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt,
2_6_29: that the LORD spoke to Moses, saying, "I am the LORD. Speak to Pharaoh king of Egypt all that I say to you."
2_6_30: But Moses said before the LORD, "Behold, I am of uncircumcised lips, and how shall Pharaoh heed me?"
2_7_1: So the LORD said to Moses: 'See, I have made you as God to Pharaoh, and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet.'
2_7_2: You shall speak all that I command you, and Aaron your brother shall tell Pharaoh to let the people of Israel go out of his land.
2_7_3: But I will harden Pharaoh's heart and multiply My signs and wonders in the land of Egypt.
2_7_4: But Pharaoh will not listen to you, so I will lay My hand on Egypt and bring out My armies and My people, the children of Israel, out of the land of Egypt by great acts of judgment.
2_7_5: The Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD, when I stretch out My hand on Egypt and bring out the children of Israel from among them.'
2_7_6: So Moses and Aaron did just as the LORD commanded them.
2_7_7: Moses was eighty years old and Aaron eighty-three when they spoke to Pharaoh.
2_7_8: Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
2_7_9: 'When Pharaoh speaks to you, saying, "Show a miracle for yourselves," then you shall say to Aaron, "Take your staff and cast it down before Pharaoh, and let it become a serpent."'
2_7_10: So Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and did just as the LORD had commanded. Aaron cast down his staff before Pharaoh and his servants, and it became a serpent.
2_7_11: But Pharaoh also called the wise men and the sorcerers; so the magicians of Egypt did the same thing by their secret arts and enchantments.
2_7_12: For each man threw down his staff, and they became serpents. But Aaron's staff swallowed up their staffs.
2_7_13: Still Pharaoh's heart was hardened, and he would not listen to them, as the LORD had said.
2_7_14: Then the LORD said to Moses: 'Pharaoh's heart is hard; he refuses to let the people go.'
2_7_15: Go to Pharaoh in the morning, when he goes out to the water, and you shall stand by the river's bank to meet him; and the staff which was turned to a serpent you shall take in your hand.
2_7_16: And you shall say to him, 'The LORD, the God of the Hebrews, has sent me to you, saying, "Let My people go, that they may serve Me in the wilderness"; but until now you would not hear.'
2_7_17: Thus says the LORD: 'By this you shall know that I am the LORD. Behold, I will strike the water in the Nile with the staff that is in my hand, and it shall be turned to blood.'
2_7_18: The fish that are in the river shall die, the river shall stink, and the Egyptians will not be able to drink the water of the river.'"
2_7_19: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, 'Say to Aaron, "Take your staff and stretch out your hand over the waters of Egypt, over their rivers, their canals, their ponds, and all their pools of water, that they may become blood. And there shall be blood throughout all the land of Egypt, both in wooden vessels and in stone vessels."'
2_7_20: So Moses and Aaron did just as the LORD commanded. He lifted up the staff and struck the water in the Nile, in the sight of Pharaoh and his servants, and all the water in the river was turned to blood.
2_7_21: The fish in the river died, the river stank, and the Egyptians could not drink the water of the river. There was blood throughout all the land of Egypt.
2_7_22: But the magicians of Egypt did the same thing by their secret arts and enchantments; so Pharaoh's heart was hardened, and he did not listen to them, as the LORD had said.
2_7_23: Then Pharaoh turned and went into his house, and he did not take even this to heart.
2_7_24: So all the Egyptians dug around the river for water to drink, because they could not drink the water of the river.
2_7_25: Seven days passed after the LORD had struck the Nile.
2_8_1: Then the LORD said to Moses, 'Go to Pharaoh and say to him, Thus says the LORD: Let My people go, that they may serve Me and worship Me.'
2_8_2: But if you refuse to let them go, behold, I will plague all your territory with frogs.
2_8_3: The Nile will swarm with frogs, which shall come up and go into your house, into your bedroom and on your bed, into the houses of your servants and on your people, and into your ovens and kneading bowls.
2_8_4: The frogs will come up on you, your people, and all your officials and servants, everywhere.
2_8_5: Then the LORD said to Moses, 'Tell Aaron: Stretch out your hand with your rod over the streams, over the rivers, and over the ponds, and cause frogs to come up on the land of Egypt.'
2_8_6: So Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt, and the frogs came up and covered the land of Egypt.
2_8_7: But the magicians did the same thing with their secret arts and enchantments, and brought up frogs on the land of Egypt.
2_8_8: Then Pharaoh called for Moses and Aaron, and said, 'Entreat the LORD that He may take away the frogs from me and from my people; and I will let the people go, that they may sacrifice to the LORD.'
2_8_9: And Moses said to Pharaoh, 'You may have the honor of appointing the time when I shall intercede for you, for your servants, and for your people, that the frogs be destroyed from you and your houses, and remain only in the river.'
2_8_10: Pharaoh said, 'Tomorrow.' Moses replied, 'Let it be according to your word, that you may know that there is no one like the LORD our God.'
2_8_11: The frogs shall depart from you, from your houses, from your servants, and from your people; they shall remain in the river only.
2_8_12: Then Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh, and Moses cried out to the LORD concerning the frogs which He had brought against Pharaoh.
2_8_13: And the LORD did according to the word of Moses. The frogs died out of the houses, out of the courtyards, and out of the fields.
2_8_14: They gathered them together in heaps, and the land stank.
2_8_15: But when Pharaoh saw that there was relief, he hardened his heart and would not listen to them, as the LORD had said.
2_8_16: Then the LORD said to Moses, 'Tell Aaron: Stretch out your staff and strike the dust of the earth, that it may become gnats throughout all the land of Egypt.'
2_8_17: And they did so. Aaron stretched out his hand with his staff and struck the dust of the earth, and gnats came on man and beast; all the dust of the land became gnats throughout all the land of Egypt.
2_8_18: The magicians tried with their secret arts and enchantments to produce gnats, but they could not. So there were gnats on man and beast.
2_8_19: Then the magicians said to Pharaoh, 'This is the finger of God.' But Pharaoh's heart was hardened, and he would not listen to them, just as the LORD had said.
2_8_20: Then the LORD said to Moses, 'Rise early in the morning and stand before Pharaoh as he comes out to the water. Say to him, Thus says the LORD: Let My people go, that they may serve Me and worship Me.'
2_8_21: For if you do not let My people go, behold, I will send swarms of flies on you and your servants, on your people and into your houses. The houses of the Egyptians shall be full of swarms of flies, and also the ground on which they stand.
2_8_22: But on that day I will set apart the land of Goshen, where My people dwell, so that no swarms of flies shall be there, in order that you may know that I am the LORD in the midst of the land.
2_8_23: I will make a distinction between My people and your people. By tomorrow this sign shall happen.'
2_8_24: And the LORD did so. Great swarms of flies came into the house of Pharaoh and into his servants' houses, and throughout all the land of Egypt the land was corrupted because of the swarms of flies.
2_8_25: Then Pharaoh called for Moses and Aaron, and said, 'Go, sacrifice to your God in the land.'
2_8_26: But Moses said, 'It is not right to do so, for the sacrifices we shall offer to the LORD our God are an abomination to the Egyptians. If we sacrifice what is detestable to the Egyptians before their eyes, will they not stone us?'
2_8_27: We must go a three days' journey into the wilderness and sacrifice to the LORD our God as He commands us.'
2_8_28: So Pharaoh said, 'I will let you go, that you may sacrifice to the LORD your God in the wilderness; only you shall not go very far away. Pray for me.'
2_8_29: Then Moses said, 'Behold, I am going out from you, and I will entreat the LORD, that the swarms of flies may depart from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people tomorrow. But let Pharaoh not deal deceitfully anymore in not letting the people go to sacrifice to the LORD.'
2_8_30: So Moses went out from Pharaoh and entreated the LORD.
2_8_31: And the LORD did according to the word of Moses; He removed the swarms of flies from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people. Not one remained.
2_8_32: But Pharaoh hardened his heart at this time also, and would not let the people go.
2_9_1: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Go in to Pharaoh and tell him, 'Thus says the LORD, the God of the Hebrews: Let my people go, that they may serve me."
2_9_2: For if you refuse to let them go and continue to hold them,
2_9_3: behold, the hand of the LORD will fall with a very severe pestilence on your livestock in the field—the horses, donkeys, camels, herds, and flocks—a deadly plague."
2_9_4: But the LORD will make a distinction between the livestock of Israel and the livestock of Egypt, so that nothing will die of all that belongs to the Israelites."
2_9_5: The LORD set a definite time, saying, 'Tomorrow the LORD will do this thing in the land.'"
2_9_6: So the LORD did this thing on the next day, and all the livestock of Egypt died; but of the livestock of the Israelites, not one died.
2_9_7: Then Pharaoh sent and found that not even one of the livestock of the Israelites was dead. But the heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he did not let the people go.
2_9_8: So the LORD said to Moses and Aaron, "Take for yourselves handfuls of soot from a furnace kiln, and let Moses scatter it toward the heavens in the sight of Pharaoh."
2_9_9: And it will become fine dust in all the land of Egypt, and it will cause festering boils breaking out in sores on man and beast throughout all the land of Egypt."
2_9_10: Then they took soot from the furnace and stood before Pharaoh, and Moses scattered it toward heaven. And it became boils breaking out in sores on man and beast.
2_9_11: And the magicians could not stand before Moses because of the boils, for the boils were on the magicians and on all the Egyptians.
2_9_12: But the LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh, and he did not listen to them, just as the LORD had spoken to Moses.
2_9_13: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Rise early in the morning and stand before Pharaoh, and say to him, 'Thus says the LORD, the God of the Hebrews: Let my people go, that they may serve me."
2_9_14: For this time I will send all my plagues on you yourself, on your servants, and on your people, so that you may know that there is no one like me in all the earth."
2_9_15: For by now I could have put forth my hand and struck you and your people with pestilence, and you would have been cut off from the earth."
2_9_16: But indeed for this purpose I have raised you up, in order to show you my power and in order to proclaim my name through all the earth."
2_9_17: As yet you exalt yourself against my people in that you will not let them go."
2_9_18: Behold, tomorrow about this time I will send very heavy hail, such as has not been in Egypt since its founding until now."
2_9_19: Therefore send now and gather your livestock and all that you have in the field to a safe place, for the hail shall come down on every man and animal which is found in the field and is not brought home; and they shall die."
2_9_20: He who feared the word of the LORD among the servants of Pharaoh made his servants and his livestock flee to the houses.
2_9_21: But he who did not regard the word of the LORD left his servants and his livestock in the field.
2_9_22: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Stretch out your hand toward heaven, that there may be hail in all the land of Egypt—on man, on beast, and on every herb of the field, throughout the land of Egypt."
2_9_23: And Moses stretched out his rod toward heaven; and the LORD sent thunder and hail, and fire ran down to the earth. And the LORD rained hail on the land of Egypt.
2_9_24: So there was very severe hail and fire flashing continually in the midst of it, such as had not been in all the land of Egypt since it became a nation.
2_9_25: And the hail struck throughout the whole land of Egypt, all that was in the field, both man and beast; and the hail struck every herb of the field and broke every tree of the field.
2_9_26: Only in the land of Goshen, where the children of Israel were, there was no hail.
2_9_27: And Pharaoh sent and called for Moses and Aaron, and said to them, "I have sinned this time. The LORD is righteous, and my people and I are wicked."
2_9_28: Entreat the LORD, that there may be no more mighty thundering and hail, for it is enough. I will let you go, and you shall stay no longer."
