2_9_29: So Moses said to him, "As soon as I have gone out of the city, I will spread out my hands to the LORD; the thunder will cease, and there will be no more hail, that you may know that the earth is the LORD's."
2_9_30: But as for you and your servants, I know that you will not yet fear the LORD God."
2_9_31: Now the flax and the barley were struck, for the barley was in the head and the flax was in bud.
2_9_32: But the wheat and the spelt were not struck, for they are late crops.
2_9_33: So Moses went out of the city from Pharaoh and spread out his hands to the LORD; then the thunder and the hail ceased, and the rain was not poured on the earth.
2_9_34: And when Pharaoh saw that the rain, the hail, and the thunder had ceased, he sinned yet more; and he hardened his heart, he and his servants.
2_9_35: So the heart of Pharaoh was hard; neither would he let the children of Israel go, as the LORD had spoken by Moses.
2_10_1: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Go to Pharaoh, for I have hardened his heart and the hearts of his officials, so that I may display these miraculous signs of Mine among them,"
2_10_2: and that you may tell in the hearing of your son and your grandson how I have dealt harshly with the Egyptians and the signs I have done among them, that you may know that I am the LORD."
2_10_3: So Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and said to him, "Thus says the LORD, the God of the Hebrews: 'How long will you refuse to humble yourself before Me? Let My people go, so that they may serve Me."
2_10_4: Or else, if you refuse to let My people go, behold, tomorrow I will bring locusts into your territory."
2_10_5: They shall cover the surface of the land so that no one will be able to see the ground. They will devour the remainder of what has escaped—what is left to you from the hail—and they will eat every tree which grows up for you out of the field."
2_10_6: They shall fill your houses, the houses of all your officials, and the houses of all the Egyptians—which neither your fathers nor your grandfathers have seen, since the day they came upon the earth until this day."' And he turned and went out from Pharaoh.
2_10_7: Then Pharaoh's officials said to him, "How long will this man be a snare to us? Let the men go, so that they may worship the LORD their God. Do you not realize that Egypt is destroyed?"
2_10_8: So Moses and Aaron were brought back to Pharaoh, and he said to them, "Go, serve the LORD your God. But who exactly are the ones that are going?"
2_10_9: Moses said, "We will go with our young and our old; with our sons and with our daughters, with our flocks and with our herds we will go, for we must hold the LORD's festival."
2_10_10: Pharaoh said to them, "The LORD be with you if I let you go with your little ones! Look, you have an evil purpose in mind."
2_10_11: Not so! Go now, the men among you, and serve the LORD, for that is what you desire." And they were driven out from Pharaoh's presence.
2_10_12: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Stretch out your hand over the land of Egypt for the locusts, so that they may come upon the land of Egypt and eat every plant of the land—all that the hail has left."
2_10_13: So Moses stretched out his staff over the land of Egypt, and the LORD brought an east wind upon the land all that day and all that night. When it was morning, the east wind had brought the locusts.
2_10_14: The locusts went up over all the land of Egypt and settled on the whole territory of Egypt. Never before had there been such a large number of locusts, nor will there be such again.
2_10_15: For they covered the surface of the whole land so that the land was darkened, and they ate every plant of the land and all the fruit of the trees that the hail had left. Nothing green was left on the trees or the plants of the field throughout all the land of Egypt.
2_10_16: Then Pharaoh hastily called for Moses and Aaron, and he said, "I have sinned against the LORD your God and against you."
2_10_17: Now therefore, please forgive my sin only this once, and entreat the LORD your God, that He may take away this death from me."
2_10_18: So he went out from Pharaoh and pleaded with the LORD.
2_10_19: And the LORD turned a very strong west wind, which took up the locusts and drove them into the Red Sea. There remained not one locust in all the territory of Egypt.
2_10_20: But the LORD hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not let the children of Israel go.
2_10_21: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Stretch out your hand toward heaven, that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt, a darkness which may even be felt."
2_10_22: So Moses stretched out his hand toward heaven, and there was thick darkness in all the land of Egypt for three days.
2_10_23: They did not see one another, nor did anyone rise from his place for three days; but all the children of Israel had light in their dwellings.
2_10_24: Then Pharaoh called to Moses and said, "Go, serve the LORD. Only let your flocks and your herds be kept back. Let your little ones also go with you."
2_10_25: But Moses said, "You must also give us sacrifices and burnt offerings, that we may sacrifice to the LORD our God."
2_10_26: Our livestock also shall go with us; not a hoof shall be left behind, for we must take some of them to serve the LORD our God, and we will not know with what we must serve the LORD until we arrive there."
2_10_27: But the LORD hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he was not willing to let them go.
2_10_28: Then Pharaoh said to him, "Get away from me! Take heed to yourself and see my face no more! For in the day you see my face you shall die!"
2_10_29: Moses said, "You have spoken well. I will never see your face again."
2_11_1: And the LORD said to Moses, "I will bring one more plague on Pharaoh and on Egypt. Afterward he will let you go from here. When he lets you go, he will surely drive you out of here altogether."
2_11_2: Speak now in the hearing of the people, and let every man ask from his neighbor and every woman from her neighbor for articles of silver and articles of gold."
2_11_3: And the LORD gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians. Moreover, the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt, in the sight of Pharaoh's servants and in the sight of the people.
2_11_4: Then Moses said, "Thus says the LORD: 'About midnight I will go out into the midst of Egypt,"
2_11_5: and all the firstborn in the land of Egypt shall die, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sits on his throne, even to the firstborn of the female servant who is behind the handmill, and all the firstborn of the animals."
2_11_6: Then there shall be a great cry throughout all the land of Egypt, such as was not like it before, nor shall be like it again."
2_11_7: But against none of the children of Israel shall a dog move its tongue, against man or beast, that you may know that the LORD makes a distinction between the Egyptians and Israel.'"
2_11_8: And all these your servants shall come down to me and bow down to me, saying, 'Get out, you and all the people who follow you!' After that I will go out." Then he went out from Pharaoh in hot anger.
2_11_9: But the LORD said to Moses, "Pharaoh will not heed you, so that My wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt."
2_11_10: So Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh; and the LORD hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not let the children of Israel go out of his land.
2_12_1: Now the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt, saying,
2_12_2: This month shall be your beginning of months; it shall be the first month of the year to you.
2_12_3: Speak to all the congregation of Israel, saying: On the tenth of this month every man shall take for himself a lamb according to the house of his father, a lamb for a household.
2_12_4: If the household is too small for the lamb, let him and his neighbor next to his house take it according to the number of the persons; according to what each man should eat, you shall make your count for the lamb.
2_12_5: Your lamb shall be without blemish, a male of the first year. You may take it from the sheep or from the goats.
2_12_6: Now you shall keep it until the fourteenth day of the same month. Then the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel shall kill it at twilight.
2_12_7: And they shall take some of the blood and put it on the two doorposts and on the lintel of the houses where they eat it.
2_12_8: Then they shall eat the flesh on that night; roasted in fire, with unleavened bread and with bitter herbs they shall eat it.
2_12_9: Do not eat it raw, nor boiled at all with water, but roasted in fire—its head with its legs and its entrails.
2_12_10: You shall let none of it remain until morning, and what remains of it until morning you shall burn with fire.
2_12_11: And thus you shall eat it: with a belt on your waist, your sandals on your feet, and your staff in your hand. So you shall eat it in haste. It is the LORD's Passover.
2_12_12: For I will pass through the land of Egypt on that night, and will strike all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgment: I am the LORD.
2_12_13: Now the blood shall be a sign for you on the houses where you are. And when I see the blood, I will pass over you; and the plague shall not be on you to destroy you when I strike the land of Egypt.
2_12_14: So this day shall be to you a memorial; and you shall keep it as a feast to the LORD throughout your generations. You shall keep it as a feast by an everlasting ordinance.
2_12_15: Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread. On the first day you shall remove leaven from your houses. For whoever eats leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day, that person shall be cut off from Israel.
2_12_16: On the first day there shall be a holy convocation, and on the seventh day there shall be a holy convocation for you. No manner of work shall be done on them; but that which everyone must eat—that only may be prepared by you.
2_12_17: So you shall observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread, for on this same day I will have brought your armies out of the land of Egypt. Therefore you shall observe this day throughout your generations as an everlasting ordinance.
2_12_18: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at evening, you shall eat unleavened bread, until the twenty-first day of the month at evening.
2_12_19: For seven days no leaven shall be found in your houses, since whoever eats what is leavened, that same person shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel, whether he is a stranger or a native of the land.
2_12_20: You shall eat nothing leavened; in all your dwellings you shall eat unleavened bread.'
2_12_21: Then Moses called for all the elders of Israel and said to them, 'Pick out and take lambs for yourselves according to your families, and kill the Passover lamb.
2_12_22: And you shall take a bunch of hyssop, dip it in the blood that is in the basin, and strike the lintel and the two doorposts with the blood that is in the basin. And none of you shall go out of the door of his house until morning.
2_12_23: For the LORD will pass through to strike the Egyptians; and when He sees the blood on the lintel and on the two doorposts, the LORD will pass over the door and not allow the destroyer to come into your houses to strike you.
2_12_24: And you shall observe this thing as an ordinance for you and your sons forever.
2_12_25: It will come to pass when you come to the land which the LORD will give you, just as He promised, that you shall keep this service.
2_12_26: And it shall be, when your children say to you, 'What do you mean by this service?'
2_12_27: that you shall say, 'It is the Passover sacrifice of the LORD, who passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt when He struck the Egyptians and delivered our households.' So the people bowed their heads and worshiped.
2_12_28: Then the children of Israel went away and did so; just as the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.
2_12_29: And it came to pass at midnight that the LORD struck all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sat on his throne to the firstborn of the captive who was in the dungeon, and all the firstborn of livestock.
2_12_30: So Pharaoh rose in the night, he, all his servants, and all the Egyptians; and there was a great cry in Egypt, for there was not a house where there was not one dead.
2_12_31: Then he called for Moses and Aaron by night, and said, 'Rise, go out from among my people, both you and the children of Israel. And go, serve the LORD as you have said.
2_12_32: Also take your flocks and your herds, as you have said, and be gone; and bless me also.'
2_12_33: And the Egyptians urged the people, that they might send them out of the land in haste. For they said, 'We shall all be dead.'
2_12_34: So the people took their dough before it was leavened, having their kneading bowls bound up in their clothes on their shoulders.
2_12_35: Now the children of Israel had done according to the word of Moses, and they had asked from the Egyptians articles of silver, articles of gold, and clothing.
2_12_36: And the LORD had given the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians, so that they granted them what they requested. Thus they plundered the Egyptians.
2_12_37: Then the children of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand men on foot, besides children.
2_12_38: A mixed multitude went up with them also, and flocks and herds—a great deal of livestock.
2_12_39: And they baked unleavened cakes of the dough which they had brought out of Egypt; for it was not leavened, because they were driven out of Egypt and could not wait, nor had they prepared provisions for themselves.
2_12_40: Now the sojourn of the children of Israel who lived in Egypt was four hundred and thirty years.
2_12_41: And it came to pass at the end of the four hundred and thirty years—on that very same day—it came to pass that all the armies of the LORD went out from the land of Egypt.
2_12_42: It is a night of solemn observance to the LORD for bringing them out of the land of Egypt. This is that night of the LORD, a solemn observance for all the children of Israel throughout their generations.
2_12_43: And the LORD said to Moses and Aaron, 'This is the ordinance of the Passover: No foreigner shall eat it.
2_12_44: But every man's servant who is bought for money, when you have circumcised him, then he may eat it.
2_12_45: A sojourner and a hired servant shall not eat it.
2_12_46: It shall be eaten in one house; you shall not carry any of the flesh outside the house, nor shall you break any of its bones.
2_12_47: All the congregation of Israel shall keep it.
2_12_48: And when a stranger dwells with you and wants to keep the Passover to the LORD, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and keep it; and he shall be as a native of the land. For no uncircumcised person shall eat it.
2_12_49: One law shall be for the native-born and for the stranger who dwells among you.
2_12_50: Thus all the children of Israel did; as the LORD commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.
2_12_51: And on that very same day the LORD brought the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt according to their armies.
2_13_1: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
2_13_2: "Consecrate to Me all the firstborn, whatever opens the womb among the children of Israel, both of man and beast; it is Mine."
2_13_3: And Moses said to the people: "Remember this day in which you went out of Egypt, out of the house of bondage; for by strength of hand the LORD brought you out of this place. No leavened bread shall be eaten."
2_13_4: Today, in the month of Abib, you are going out.
2_13_5: And when the LORD brings you into the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Hivites and the Jebusites, which He swore to your fathers to give you, a land flowing with milk and honey, you shall keep this service in this month.
2_13_6: For seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on the seventh day there shall be a feast to the LORD.
2_13_7: Unleavened bread shall be eaten throughout the seven days. No leavened bread shall be seen with you, nor shall any leaven be seen among you in all your quarters.
2_13_8: And you shall tell your son in that day, saying, 'This is done because of what the LORD did for me when I came out of Egypt.'"
2_13_9: It shall serve as a sign on your hand and as a memorial between your eyes, that the LORD's law may be in your mouth; for with a strong hand the LORD has brought you out of Egypt.
2_13_10: Therefore, you shall keep this ordinance in its season from year to year.
2_13_11: "And when the LORD brings you into the land of the Canaanites, as He swore to you and your fathers, and gives it to you,"
2_13_12: you shall set apart to the LORD all that opens the womb, that is, every firstborn from your animals; the males shall be the LORD's.
2_13_13: But every firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb; and if you will not redeem it, then you shall break its neck. And every firstborn of man among your sons you shall redeem.
2_13_14: So it shall be, when your son asks you in time to come, saying, 'What is this?' that you shall say to him, 'By strength of hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt, out of the house of bondage."
2_13_15: When Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, the LORD killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both the firstborn of man and the firstborn of beast. Therefore I sacrifice to the LORD all males that open the womb, but all the firstborn of my sons I redeem.'"
2_13_16: It shall be as a sign on your hand and as frontlets between your eyes, for by strength of hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt."
2_13_17: When Pharaoh had let the people go, God did not lead them by way of the land of the Philistines, although that was near; for God said, "Lest the people change their minds when they see war, and return to Egypt."
2_13_18: So God led the people around by the way of the wilderness toward the Red Sea. And the children of Israel went up in orderly ranks out of the land of Egypt.
2_13_19: And Moses took the bones of Joseph with him, for Joseph had placed the children of Israel under solemn oath, saying, "God will surely visit you, and you shall carry up my bones from here with you."
2_13_20: So they took their journey from Succoth and camped in Etham at the edge of the wilderness.
2_13_21: And the LORD went before them by day in a pillar of cloud to lead them along the way, and by night in a pillar of fire to give them light, so that they could travel by day and by night.
2_13_22: He did not take away the pillar of cloud by day or the pillar of fire by night from before the people.
2_14_1: Now the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
2_14_2: Speak to the children of Israel, that they turn and camp before Pi Hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, opposite Baal Zephon; you shall camp before it by the sea.
2_14_3: For Pharaoh will say of the children of Israel, 'They are bewildered by the land; the wilderness has closed them in.'
2_14_4: Then I will harden Pharaoh's heart, so that he will pursue them; and I will gain honor over Pharaoh and over all his army, that the Egyptians may know that I am the LORD. And they did so.
2_14_5: Now it was told the king of Egypt that the people had fled, and the heart of Pharaoh and his servants was turned against the people; and they said, 'Why have we done this, that we have let Israel go from serving us?'
2_14_6: So he made ready his chariot and took his people with him.
2_14_7: Also, he took six hundred choice chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt with captains over every one of them.
2_14_8: And the LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued the children of Israel; and the children of Israel went out with boldness.
2_14_9: So the Egyptians pursued them, all the horses and chariots of Pharaoh, his horsemen and his army, and overtook them camping by the sea beside Pi Hahiroth, before Baal Zephon.
2_14_10: And when Pharaoh drew near, the children of Israel lifted their eyes, and behold, the Egyptians marched after them. So they were very afraid, and the children of Israel cried out to the LORD.
2_14_11: Then they said to Moses, 'Because there were no graves in Egypt, have you taken us away to die in the wilderness? Why have you so dealt with us, to bring us up out of Egypt?'
2_14_12: Is this not the word that we told you in Egypt, saying, 'Let us alone that we may serve the Egyptians'? For it would have been better for us to serve the Egyptians than that we should die in the wilderness.'
2_14_13: And Moses said to the people, 'Do not be afraid. Stand still, and see the salvation of the LORD, which He will accomplish for you today. For the Egyptians whom you see today, you shall see again no more forever.'
2_14_14: The LORD will fight for you, and you shall hold your peace.
2_14_15: And the LORD said to Moses, 'Why do you cry to Me? Tell the children of Israel to go forward.'
2_14_16: But lift up your rod, and stretch out your hand over the sea and divide it. And the children of Israel shall go on dry ground through the midst of the sea.
2_14_17: And I indeed will harden the hearts of the Egyptians, and they shall follow them. So I will gain honor over Pharaoh and over all his army, his chariots, and his horsemen.
2_14_18: Then the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD, when I have gained honor for Myself over Pharaoh, his chariots, and his horsemen.
2_14_19: And the Angel of God, who went before the camp of Israel, moved and went behind them; and the pillar of cloud went from before them and stood behind them.
2_14_20: So it came between the camp of the Egyptians and the camp of Israel. Thus it was a cloud and darkness to the one, and it gave light by night to the other, so that the one did not come near the other all that night.
2_14_21: Then Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the LORD caused the sea to go back by a strong east wind all that night, and made the sea into dry land, and the waters were divided.
2_14_22: So the children of Israel went into the midst of the sea on the dry ground, and the waters were a wall to them on their right hand and on their left.
2_14_23: And the Egyptians pursued and went after them into the midst of the sea, all Pharaoh's horses, his chariots, and his horsemen.
2_14_24: Now it came to pass, in the morning watch, that the LORD looked down upon the army of the Egyptians through the pillar of fire and cloud, and He troubled the army of the Egyptians.
2_14_25: And He took off their chariot wheels, so that they drove them with difficulty; and the Egyptians said, 'Let us flee from the face of Israel, for the LORD fights for them against the Egyptians.'
2_14_26: Then the LORD said to Moses, 'Stretch out your hand over the sea, that the waters may come back upon the Egyptians, on their chariots, and on their horsemen.'
2_14_27: And Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and when the morning appeared, the sea returned to its full depth, while the Egyptians were fleeing into it. So the LORD overthrew the Egyptians in the midst of the sea.
2_14_28: Then the waters returned and covered the chariots, the horsemen, and all the army of Pharaoh that came into the sea after them. Not so much as one of them remained.
2_14_29: But the children of Israel had walked on dry land in the midst of the sea, and the waters were a wall to them on their right hand and on their left.
2_14_30: So the LORD saved Israel that day out of the hand of the Egyptians, and Israel saw the Egyptians dead on the seashore.
2_14_31: Thus Israel saw the great work which the LORD had done in Egypt; so the people feared the LORD, and believed the LORD and His servant Moses.
2_15_1: Then Moses and the children of Israel sang this song to the LORD, and spoke, saying: "I will sing to the LORD, for He has triumphed gloriously! The horse and its rider He has thrown into the sea!"
2_15_2: The LORD is my strength and my song, and He has become my salvation; this is my God, and I will praise Him; my father's God, and I will exalt Him.
2_15_3: The LORD is a warrior; the LORD is His name.
2_15_4: Pharaoh's chariots and his army He has cast into the sea; His chosen captains are drowned in the Red Sea.
2_15_5: The depths have covered them; they went down into the depths like a stone.
2_15_6: Your right hand, O LORD, is glorious in power; Your right hand, O LORD, shatters the enemy.
2_15_7: In the greatness of Your majesty You overthrow those who rise against You; You unleash Your burning wrath; it consumes them like stubble.
2_15_8: At the blast of Your nostrils the waters piled up; the floods stood up like a heap; the deeps were congealed in the heart of the sea.
2_15_9: The enemy said, 'I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil; My desire shall be satisfied against them. I will draw my sword, my hand will destroy them.'
2_15_10: You blew with Your wind, and the sea covered them; they sank like lead in the mighty waters.
2_15_11: Who is like You, O LORD, among the gods? Who is like You, majestic in holiness, awesome in glorious deeds, doing wonders?
2_15_12: You stretched out Your right hand, and the earth swallowed them.
2_15_13: In Your lovingkindness You have led the people whom You have redeemed; in Your strength You have guided them to Your holy habitation.
2_15_14: The peoples have heard and tremble; anguish has gripped the inhabitants of Philistia.
2_15_15: Then the chiefs of Edom were dismayed; trembling has seized the leaders of Moab; all the inhabitants of Canaan have melted away.
2_15_16: Terror and dread fall upon them; by the greatness of Your arm they are as still as a stone, until Your people pass over, O LORD, until the people whom You have purchased pass by.
2_15_17: You will bring them in and plant them on the mountain of Your inheritance, the place, O LORD, which You have made for Your own dwelling, the sanctuary, O Lord, which Your hands have established.
2_15_18: The LORD shall reign forever and ever.
2_15_19: For the horses of Pharaoh with his chariots and his horsemen went into the sea, and the LORD brought back the waters of the sea upon them; but the children of Israel went on dry land in the midst of the sea.
2_15_20: Then Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took the timbrel in her hand; and all the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances.
2_15_21: And Miriam answered them: "Sing to the LORD, for He has triumphed gloriously! The horse and its rider He has thrown into the sea!"
2_15_22: So Moses brought Israel from the Red Sea; then they went out into the Wilderness of Shur. And they went three days in the wilderness and found no water.
2_15_23: Now when they came to Marah, they could not drink the waters of Marah, for they were bitter. Therefore the name of it was called Marah.
2_15_24: And the people complained against Moses, saying, "What shall we drink?"
2_15_25: So he cried out to the LORD, and the LORD showed him a tree. When he cast it into the waters, the waters were made sweet. There He made a statute and an ordinance for them, and there He tested them.
2_15_26: And He said, "If you will diligently listen to the voice of the LORD your God and do what is right in His sight, give ear to His commandments and keep all His statutes, I will put none of the diseases on you which I have brought on the Egyptians, for I am the LORD who heals you."
2_15_27: Then they came to Elim, where there were twelve wells of water and seventy palm trees; so they camped there by the waters.
2_16_1: They journeyed from Elim, and all the congregation of the children of Israel came to the Wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after they departed from the land of Egypt.
2_16_2: Then the whole congregation of the children of Israel complained against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness.
2_16_3: And the children of Israel said to them, "Oh, that we had died by the hand of the LORD in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the pots of meat and ate bread to the full! For you have brought us out into this wilderness to kill this whole assembly with hunger."
2_16_4: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you. And the people shall go out and gather a day's portion every day, that I may test them, whether they will walk in My law or not."
2_16_5: And it shall be on the sixth day that they shall prepare what they bring in, and it shall be twice as much as they gather daily."
2_16_6: Then Moses and Aaron said to all the children of Israel, "At evening you shall know that the LORD has brought you out of the land of Egypt."
2_16_7: And in the morning you shall see the glory of the LORD; for He hears your complaints against the LORD. But what are we, that you complain against us?"
2_16_8: Also Moses said, "This shall be seen when the LORD gives you meat to eat in the evening, and in the morning bread to the full; for the LORD hears your complaints which you make against Him. And what are we? Your complaints are not against us but against the LORD."
2_16_9: Then Moses spoke to Aaron, "Say to all the congregation of the children of Israel, 'Come near before the LORD, for He has heard your complaints.'"
2_16_10: Now it came to pass, as Aaron spoke to the whole congregation of the children of Israel, that they looked toward the wilderness, and behold, the glory of the LORD appeared in the cloud.
2_16_11: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
2_16_12: "I have heard the complaints of the children of Israel. Speak to them, saying, 'At twilight you shall eat meat, and in the morning you shall be filled with bread. And you shall know that I am the LORD your God.'"
2_16_13: So it was that quails came up at evening and covered the camp, and in the morning the dew lay all around the camp.
2_16_14: And when the layer of dew lifted, there, on the surface of the wilderness, was a small round substance, as fine as frost on the ground.
2_16_15: So when the children of Israel saw it, they said to one another, "What is it?" For they did not know what it was. And Moses said to them, "This is the bread which the LORD has given you to eat."
2_16_16: This is the thing which the LORD has commanded: 'Let every man gather it according to each one's need, one omer for each person, according to the number of persons; let every man take for those who are in his tent.'"
2_16_17: Then the children of Israel did so and gathered, some more, some less.
2_16_18: So when they measured it by omers, he who gathered much had nothing left over, and he who gathered little had no lack. Every man had gathered according to each one's need.
2_16_19: And Moses said, "Let no one leave any of it till morning."
2_16_20: Notwithstanding they did not heed Moses. But some of them left part of it until morning, and it bred worms and stank. And Moses was angry with them.
2_16_21: So they gathered it every morning, every man according to his need. And when the sun became hot, it melted.
2_16_22: And so it was, on the sixth day, that they gathered twice as much bread, two omers for each one. And all the rulers of the congregation came and told Moses.
2_16_23: Then he said to them, "This is what the LORD has said: 'Tomorrow is a Sabbath rest, a holy Sabbath to the LORD. Bake what you will bake today, and boil what you will boil; and lay up for yourselves all that remains, to be kept until morning.'"
2_16_24: So they laid it up till morning, as Moses commanded; and it did not stink, nor were there any worms in it.
2_16_25: Then Moses said, "Eat that today, for today is a Sabbath to the LORD; today you will not find it in the field."
2_16_26: Six days you shall gather it, but on the seventh day, the Sabbath, there will be none."
2_16_27: Now it happened that some of the people went out on the seventh day to gather, but they found none.
2_16_28: And the LORD said to Moses, "How long do you refuse to keep My commandments and My laws?"
2_16_29: See! For the LORD has given you the Sabbath; therefore He gives you on the sixth day bread for two days. Let every man remain in his place; let no man go out of his place on the seventh day."
2_16_30: So the people rested on the seventh day.
2_16_31: And the house of Israel called its name Manna. And it was like white coriander seed, and the taste of it was like wafers made with honey.
2_16_32: Then Moses said, "This is the thing which the LORD has commanded: 'Fill an omer with it, to be kept for your generations, that they may see the bread with which I fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you out of the land of Egypt.'"
2_16_33: And Moses said to Aaron, "Take a pot and put an omer of manna in it, and lay it up before the LORD, to be kept for your generations."
2_16_34: As the LORD commanded Moses, so Aaron laid it up before the Testimony, to be kept.
2_16_35: And the children of Israel ate manna forty years, until they came to an inhabited land; they ate manna until they came to the border of the land of Canaan.
2_16_36: Now an omer is one-tenth of an ephah.
2_17_1: Then all the congregation of the children of Israel set out on their journey from the Wilderness of Sin by stages, according to the commandment of the LORD, and camped at Rephidim; but there was no water for the people to drink.
2_17_2: Therefore the people quarreled with Moses and said, 'Give us water that we may drink.' And Moses said to them, 'Why do you quarrel with me? Why do you test the LORD?'
2_17_3: But the people thirsted there for water; and they grumbled against Moses and said, 'Why have you brought us up from Egypt to kill us and our children and our livestock with thirst?'
2_17_4: So Moses cried out to the LORD, saying, 'What shall I do with this people? They are almost ready to stone me!'
2_17_5: And the LORD said to Moses, 'Go on before the people and take with you some of the elders of Israel. Also take in your hand your rod with which you struck the Nile, and go.'
2_17_6: Behold, I will stand before you there on the rock at Horeb; and you shall strike the rock, and water will come out of it, that the people may drink.' And Moses did so in the sight of the elders of Israel.
2_17_7: So he called the name of the place Massah and Meribah, because of the quarreling of the children of Israel, and because they tested the LORD, saying, 'Is the LORD among us or not?'
2_17_8: Then Amalek came and fought with Israel at Rephidim.
2_17_9: And Moses said to Joshua, 'Choose some men for us and go out, fight with Amalek. Tomorrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the rod of God in my hand.'
2_17_10: So Joshua did as Moses said to him, and fought with Amalek; and Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.
2_17_11: And so it was, when Moses held up his hand, that Israel prevailed; and when he let down his hand, Amalek prevailed.
2_17_12: But Moses' hands became heavy; so they took a stone and put it under him, and he sat on it. And Aaron and Hur supported his hands, one on one side and one on the other, so that his hands remained steady until the sun went down.
2_17_13: So Joshua defeated Amalek and his people with the edge of the sword.
2_17_14: Then the LORD said to Moses, 'Write this in a book as a memorial and recite it in the hearing of Joshua, that I will utterly blot out the memory of Amalek from under heaven.'
2_17_15: And Moses built an altar and called its name The-LORD-Is-My-Banner;
2_17_16: for he said, 'Because the LORD has sworn: the LORD will have war with Amalek from generation to generation.'
2_18_1: And Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses' father-in-law, heard of all that God had done for Moses and for Israel His people—how the LORD had brought Israel out of Egypt.
2_18_2: Now Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, had taken in Zipporah, Moses' wife, after he had sent her back.
2_18_3: along with her two sons, of whom one was named Gershom (for Moses had said, 'I have been a stranger in a foreign land').
2_18_4: and the other Eliezer (for he said, 'The God of my father was my help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh').
2_18_5: Then Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, came with his sons and his wife to Moses in the wilderness, where he was encamped at the mountain of God.
2_18_6: He sent word to Moses, saying, 'I, your father-in-law Jethro, am coming to you with your wife and her two sons with her.'
2_18_7: So Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, bowed down, and kissed him. They asked each other about their well-being, and they went into the tent.
2_18_8: And Moses told his father-in-law all that the LORD had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel's sake, all the hardship that had come upon them on the way, and how the LORD had delivered them.
2_18_9: Then Jethro rejoiced for all the good which the LORD had done for Israel, whom He had delivered out of the hand of the Egyptians.
2_18_10: And Jethro said, 'Blessed be the LORD, who has delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians and out of the hand of Pharaoh, and who has delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.'
2_18_11: Now I know that the LORD is greater than all the gods; for in the very thing in which they dealt proudly, He was above them.'
2_18_12: Then Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, took a burnt offering and other sacrifices to offer to God. And Aaron came with all the elders of Israel to eat bread with Moses' father-in-law before God.
2_18_13: And so it was, on the next day, that Moses sat to judge the people; and the people stood before Moses from morning until evening.
2_18_14: So when Moses' father-in-law saw all that he was doing for the people, he said, 'What is this thing that you are doing for the people? Why do you alone sit, and all the people stand before you from morning until evening?'
2_18_15: And Moses said to his father-in-law, 'Because the people come to me to inquire of God.'
2_18_16: When they have a difficulty, they come to me, and I judge between one and another; and I make known the statutes of God and His laws.'
2_18_17: So Moses' father-in-law said to him, 'The thing that you do is not good.'
2_18_18: Both you and these people who are with you will surely wear yourselves out. For this thing is too much for you; you are not able to perform it by yourself.'
2_18_19: Listen now to my voice; I will give you counsel, and God will be with you: Stand before God for the people, so that you may bring the difficulties to God.
2_18_20: And you shall teach them the statutes and the laws, and show them the way in which they must walk and the work they must do.
2_18_21: Moreover you shall select from all the people able men, such as fear God, men of truth, hating covetousness; and place such over them to be rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
2_18_22: And let them judge the people at all times. Then it will be that every great matter they shall bring to you, but every small matter they themselves shall judge. So it will be easier for you, for they will bear the burden with you.
2_18_23: If you do this thing, and God so commands you, then you will be able to endure, and all this people will also go to their place in peace.'
2_18_24: So Moses heeded the voice of his father-in-law and did all that he had said.
2_18_25: And Moses chose able men out of all Israel, and made them heads over the people: rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
2_18_26: So they judged the people at all times; the hard cases they brought to Moses, but they judged every small case themselves.
2_18_27: Then Moses let his father-in-law depart, and he went his way to his own land.
2_19_1: In the third month after the children of Israel had gone out of the land of Egypt, on that very day they came into the Wilderness of Sinai.
2_19_2: When they had departed from Rephidim, they came to the wilderness of Sinai and camped in the wilderness; there Israel camped in front of the mountain.
2_19_3: Moses went up to God, and the LORD called to him from the mountain, saying, 'Thus you shall say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel:'
2_19_4: 'You have seen what I did to the Egyptians, and how I bore you on eagles' wings and brought you to Myself.'
2_19_5: 'Now therefore, if you will indeed obey My voice and keep My covenant, then you shall be My own special possession among all peoples, for all the earth is Mine.'
2_19_6: 'And you shall be to Me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation.' These are the words which you shall speak to the children of Israel."
2_19_7: So Moses came and called for the elders of the people, and set before them all these words which the LORD commanded him.
2_19_8: Then all the people answered together and said, 'All that the LORD has spoken we will do.' And Moses brought back the words of the people to the LORD.
2_19_9: And the LORD said to Moses, 'Behold, I come to you in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with you, and believe you forever.' So Moses told the words of the people to the LORD.
2_19_10: Then the LORD said to Moses, 'Go to the people and consecrate them today and tomorrow, and let them wash their clothes.'
2_19_11: 'And let them be ready for the third day, for on the third day the LORD will come down upon Mount Sinai in the sight of all the people.'
2_19_12: 'You shall set bounds for the people all around, saying, "Take heed to yourselves that you do not go up to the mountain or touch its base. Whoever touches the mountain shall surely be put to death.'
2_19_13: 'No hand shall touch him, but he shall surely be stoned or shot with an arrow; whether man or beast, he shall not live.' When the trumpet sounds long, they shall come near the mountain."
2_19_14: So Moses went down from the mountain to the people and consecrated the people, and they washed their clothes.
2_19_15: And he said to the people, 'Be ready for the third day; do not come near your wives.'
2_19_16: Then it came to pass on the third day, in the morning, that there were thunderings and lightnings, and a thick cloud on the mountain; and the sound of the trumpet was very loud, so that all the people who were in the camp trembled.
2_19_17: And Moses brought the people out of the camp to meet with God, and they stood at the foot of the mountain.
2_19_18: Now Mount Sinai was completely in smoke, because the LORD descended upon it in fire. Its smoke ascended like the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mountain quaked greatly.
2_19_19: And when the blast of the trumpet sounded long and grew louder and louder, Moses spoke, and God answered him with thunder.
2_19_20: Then the LORD came down upon Mount Sinai, on the top of the mountain. And the LORD called Moses to the top of the mountain, and Moses went up.
2_19_21: And the LORD said to Moses, 'Go down and warn the people, lest they break through to gaze at the LORD, and many of them perish.'
2_19_22: 'Also let the priests who come near the LORD consecrate themselves, lest the LORD break out against them.'"
2_19_23: But Moses said to the LORD, 'The people cannot come up to Mount Sinai; for You warned us, saying, "Set bounds around the mountain and consecrate it."'
2_19_24: Then the LORD said to him, 'Go down and come up again, you and Aaron with you; but do not let the priests and the people break through to come up to the LORD, lest He break out against them.'
2_19_25: So Moses went down to the people and spoke to them.
2_20_1: And God spoke all these words, saying:
2_20_2: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.
2_20_3: You shall have no other gods before Me.
2_20_4: You shall not make for yourself a carved image or any likeness of anything that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth.
2_20_5: You shall not bow down to them nor serve them; for I, the LORD your God, am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children to the third and fourth generations of those who hate Me.
2_20_6: But showing mercy to thousands, to those who love Me and keep My commandments.
2_20_7: You shall not take the name of the LORD your God in vain, for the LORD will not hold him guiltless who takes His name in vain.
2_20_8: Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.
2_20_9: Six days you shall labor and do all your work,
2_20_10: But the seventh day is the Sabbath of the LORD your God. In it you shall do no work: you, nor your son, nor your daughter, nor your male servant, nor your female servant, nor your cattle, nor your stranger who is within your gates.
2_20_11: For in six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested the seventh day. Therefore the LORD blessed the Sabbath day and hallowed it.
2_20_12: Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long upon the land which the LORD your God is giving you.
2_20_16: You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
2_20_17: You shall not covet your neighbor's house; you shall not covet your neighbor's wife, nor his male servant, nor his female servant, nor his ox, nor his donkey, nor anything that is your neighbor's.
2_20_18: Now all the people witnessed the thunderings, the lightning flashes, the sound of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking; and when the people saw it, they trembled and stood afar off.
2_20_19: Then they said to Moses, "You speak with us, and we will hear; but let not God speak with us, lest we die."
2_20_20: And Moses said to the people, "Do not fear; for God has come to test you, and that His fear may be before you, so that you may not sin."
2_20_21: So the people stood afar off, but Moses drew near the thick darkness where God was.
2_20_22: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Thus you shall say to the children of Israel: 'You have seen that I have talked with you from heaven."
2_20_23: You shall not make anything to be with Me—gods of silver or gods of gold you shall not make for yourselves.
2_20_24: An altar of earth you shall make for Me, and you shall sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your oxen. In every place where I record My name I will come to you, and I will bless you.
2_20_25: And if you make Me an altar of stone, you shall not build it of hewn stone; for if you use your tool on it, you have profaned it.
2_20_26: Nor shall you go up by steps to My altar, that your nakedness may not be exposed on it.
